               found_inf=getattr(self, \"found_inf\", None),\n                decoupled_weight_decay=group[\"decoupled_weight_decay\"],\n            )\n\n        return loss\n\n\nAdam.__doc__ = (\n    r\"\"\"Implements Adam algorithm.\n\n    .. math::\n       \\begin{aligned}\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n            &\\textbf{input}      : \\gamma \\text{ (lr)}, \\beta_1, \\beta_2\n                \\text{ (betas)},\\theta_0 \\text{ (params)},f(\\theta) \\text{ (objective)}          \\\\\n            &\\hspace{13mm}      \\lambda \\text{ (weight decay)},  \\: \\textit{amsgrad},\n                \\:\\textit{maximize},  \\: \\epsilon \\text{ (epsilon)}                              \\\\\n            &\\textbf{initialize} :  m_0 \\leftarrow 0 \\text{ ( first moment)},\n                v_0\\leftarrow 0 \\text{ (second moment)},\\: v_0^{max}\\leftarrow 0          \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n            &\\textbf{for} \\: t=1 \\: \\textbf{to} \\: \\ldots \\: \\textbf{do}                         \\\\\n\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\textit{maximize}:                                       \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   -\\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})         \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{else}                                                           \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   \\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})          \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\lambda \\neq 0                                           \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm} g_t \\leftarrow g_t + \\lambda  \\theta_{t-1}                            \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}m_t           \\leftarrow   \\beta_1 m_{t-1} + (1 - \\beta_1) g_t          \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}v_t           \\leftarrow   \\beta_2 v_{t-1} + (1-\\beta_2) g^2_t          \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\widehat{m_t} \\leftarrow   m_t/\\big(1-\\beta_1^t \\big)                   \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{if} \\: amsgrad                                                  \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm} v_t^{max} \\leftarrow \\mathrm{max}(v_{t-1}^{max},v_t)                  \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm}\\widehat{v_t} \\leftarrow v_t^{max}/\\big(1-\\beta_2^t \\big)              \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{else}                                                           \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm}\\widehat{v_t} \\leftarrow   v_t/\\big(1-\\beta_2^t \\big)                  \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\theta_t \\leftarrow \\theta_{t-1} - \\gamma \\widehat{m_t}/\n                \\big(\\sqrt{\\widehat{v_t}} + \\epsilon \\big)                                       \\\\\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\bf{return} \\:  \\theta_t                                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n       \\end{aligned}\n\n    For further details regarding the algorithm we refer to `Adam: A Method for Stochastic Optimization`_.\n    \"\"\"\n    + rf\"\"\"\n    Args:\n        {_params_doc}\n        lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 1e-3). A tensor LR\n            is not yet supported for all our implementations. Please use a float\n            LR if you are not also specifying fused=True or capturable=True.\n        betas (Tuple[float, float], optional): coefficients used for computing\n            running averages of gradient and its square (default: (0.9, 0.999))\n        eps (float, optional): term added to the denominator to improve\n            numerical stability (default: 1e-8)\n        weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay (L2 penalty) (default: 0)\n        decoupled_weight_decay (bool, optional): if True, this optimizer is\n            equivalent to AdamW and the algorithm will not accumulate weight\n            decay in the momentum nor variance. (default: False)\n        amsgrad (bool, optional): whether to use the AMSGrad variant of this\n            algorithm from the paper `On the Convergence of Adam and Beyond`_\n            (default: False)\n        {_foreach_doc}\n        {_maximize_doc}\n        {_capturable_doc}\n        {_differentiable_doc}\n        {_fused_doc}\n    .. Note::\n        A prototype implementation of Adam and AdamW for MPS supports `torch.float32` and `torch.float16`.\n    .. _Adam\\: A Method for Stochastic Optimization:\n        https://arxiv.org/abs/1412.6980\n    .. _On the Convergence of Adam and Beyond:\n        https://openreview.net/forum?id=ryQu7f-RZ\n\n    \"\"\"\n)\n\n\ndef _single_tensor_adam(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    exp_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],\n    max_exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\n    *,\n    amsgrad: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n    beta1: Union[float, Tensor],\n    beta2: Union[float, Tensor],\n    lr: Union[float, Tensor],\n    weight_decay: float,\n    eps: float,\n    maximize: bool,\n    capturable: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    decoupled_weight_decay: bool,\n):\n    assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None\n\n    if torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        # this assert is due to JIT being dumb and not realizing that the ops below\n        # have overloads to handle both float and Tensor lrs, so we just assert it's\n        # a float since most people using JIT are using floats\n        assert isinstance(lr, float)\n        assert isinstance(beta1, float)\n        assert isinstance(beta2, float)\n    else:\n        lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n        # TODO: Support nonzero-dim Tensor betas, see #147921\n\n    # We only shuffle around the beta when it is a Tensor, otherwise, we prefer\n    # treating it as a scalar.\n    # Note: ensure type declaration is under conditional check for isinstance\n    # or else torchscript will get cranky about the DeviceDict type.\n    if isinstance(beta1, Tensor):\n        beta1_dict: Optional[DeviceDtypeDict] = {(beta1.device, beta1.dtype): beta1}\n    else:\n        beta1_dict = None\n\n    for i, param in enumerate(params):\n        grad = grads[i] if not maximize else -grads[i]\n        exp_avg = exp_avgs[i]\n        exp_avg_sq = exp_avg_sqs[i]\n        step_t = state_steps[i]\n\n        # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n            capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices()\n            assert (\n                param.device.type == step_t.device.type\n                and param.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n            ), (\n                f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n            )\n\n        # update step\n        step_t += 1\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            if decoupled_weight_decay:\n                # Perform stepweight decay\n                param.mul_(1 - lr * weight_decay)\n            else:\n                # Nested if is necessary to bypass jitscript rules\n                if differentiable and isinstance(weight_decay, Tensor):\n                    if weight_decay.requires_grad:\n                        grad = grad.addcmul_(param.clone(), weight_decay)\n                    else:\n                        grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\n                else:\n                    grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\n\n        if torch.is_complex(param):\n            grad = torch.view_as_real(grad)\n            exp_avg = torch.view_as_real(exp_avg)\n            exp_avg_sq = torch.view_as_real(exp_avg_sq)\n            if amsgrad:\n                max_exp_avg_sqs[i] = torch.view_as_real(max_exp_avg_sqs[i])\n            param = torch.view_as_real(param)\n\n        device = param.device\n\n        if beta1_dict is not None:\n            dtype = param.dtype  # type: ignore[union-attr]\n\n            # cast to workaround https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/140601\n            key = (device, dtype)\n            if key not in beta1_dict:\n                beta1_dict[key] = beta1.to(  # type: ignore[union-attr]\n                    device=device, dtype=dtype, non_blocking=True\n                )\n\n            device_beta1: Union[float, Tensor] = beta1_dict[key]\n        else:\n            device_beta1 = beta1\n\n        # Decay the first and second moment running average coefficient\n        exp_avg.lerp_(grad, 1 - device_beta1)\n\n        # Nested if is necessary to bypass jitscript rules\n        if differentiable and isinstance(beta2, Tensor):\n            if beta2.requires_grad:\n                # Using lerp to only use 2 operations bc addcmul's value cannot be a tensor\n                # Showing equivalence of differentiable path and nondifferentiable path\n                # expavg * b2 + grad^2 * (1-b2)\n                #           add expavg * (1-b2) - expavg * (1-b2) = 0\n                # expavg * b2 + expavg * (1-b2) - expavg * (1-b2) + grad^2 * (1-b2)\n                # expavg - expavg * (1-b2) + grad^2 * (1-b2)\n                # expavg + (grad^2 - expavg) * (1-b2)\n                # expavg.lerp(grad^2, 1-beta2)\n                exp_avg_sq.lerp_(torch.square(grad), weight=1 - beta2)\n            else:\n                exp_avg_sq.mul_(beta2).addcmul_(\n                    grad, grad, value=cast(float, 1 - beta2)\n                )\n        else:\n            exp_avg_sq.mul_(beta2).addcmul_(grad, grad, value=1 - beta2)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n\n        if capturable or differentiable:\n            step = step_t\n\n            # Nested if is necessary to bypass jitscript rules\n            if differentiable and isinstance(beta1, Tensor):\n                if beta1.requires_grad:\n                    bias_correction1 = 1 - beta1 ** step.clone()\n                else:\n                    bias_correction1 = 1 - beta1**step\n            else:\n                bias_correction1 = 1 - beta1**step\n\n            # Nested if is necessary to bypass jitscript rules\n            if differentiable and isinstance(beta2, Tensor):\n                if beta2.requires_grad:\n                    bias_correction2 = 1 - beta2 ** step.clone()\n                else:\n                    bias_correction2 = 1 - beta2**step\n            else:\n                bias_correction2 = 1 - beta2**step\n\n            step_size = lr / bias_correction1\n            step_size_neg = step_size.neg()\n\n            bias_correction2_sqrt = bias_correction2.sqrt()\n\n            if amsgrad:\n                # Maintains the maximum of all 2nd moment running avg. till now\n                if differentiable:\n                    max_exp_avg_sq = max_exp_avg_sqs[i].clone()\n                else:\n                    max_exp_avg_sq = max_exp_avg_sqs[i]\n\n                max_exp_avg_sqs[i].copy_(torch.maximum(max_exp_avg_sq, exp_avg_sq))\n\n                # Uses the max. for normalizing running avg. of gradient\n                # Folds in (admittedly ugly) 1-elem step_size math here to avoid extra param-set-sized read+write\n                # (can't fold it into addcdiv_ below because addcdiv_ requires value is a Number, not a Tensor)\n                denom = (\n                    max_exp_avg_sqs[i].sqrt() / (bias_correction2_sqrt * step_size_neg)\n                ).add_(eps / step_size_neg)\n            else:\n                denom = (\n                    exp_avg_sq.sqrt() / (bias_correction2_sqrt * step_size_neg)\n                ).add_(eps / step_size_neg)\n\n            if differentiable:\n                param.addcdiv_(exp_avg.clone(), denom)\n            else:\n                param.addcdiv_(exp_avg, denom)\n        else:\n            step = _get_value(step_t)\n\n            bias_correction1 = 1 - beta1**step\n            bias_correction2 = 1 - beta2**step\n\n            step_size = lr / bias_correction1\n\n            bias_correction2_sqrt = bias_correction2**0.5\n\n            if amsgrad:\n                # Maintains the maximum of all 2nd moment running avg. till now\n                torch.maximum(max_exp_avg_sqs[i], exp_avg_sq, out=max_exp_avg_sqs[i])\n\n                # Use the max. for normalizing running avg. of gradient\n                denom = (max_exp_avg_sqs[i].sqrt() / bias_correction2_sqrt).add_(eps)\n            else:\n                denom = (exp_avg_sq.sqrt() / bias_correction2_sqrt).add_(eps)\n\n            param.addcdiv_(exp_avg, denom, value=-step_size)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n\n        # Lastly, switch back to complex view\n        if amsgrad and torch.is_complex(params[i]):\n            max_exp_avg_sqs[i] = torch.view_as_complex(max_exp_avg_sqs[i])\n\n\ndef _multi_tensor_adam(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    exp_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],\n    max_exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\n    *,\n    amsgrad: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n    beta1: Union[float, Tensor],\n    beta2: Union[float, Tensor],\n    lr: Union[float, Tensor],\n    weight_decay: float,\n    eps: float,\n    maximize: bool,\n    capturable: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    decoupled_weight_decay: bool,\n):\n    if len(params) == 0:\n        return\n\n    if isinstance(lr, Tensor):\n        if not capturable:\n            raise RuntimeError(\n                \"lr as a Tensor is not supported for capturable=False and foreach=True\"\n            )\n        if lr.numel() != 1:\n            raise ValueError(\"Tensor lr must be 1-element\")\n\n    if isinstance(beta1, Tensor):\n        if not capturable:\n            raise ValueError(\n                \"beta1 as a Tensor is not supported for capturable=False and foreach=True\"\n            )\n        if beta1.numel() != 1:\n            raise ValueError(\"Tensor beta1 must be 1-element\")\n\n    if isinstance(beta2, Tensor):\n        if not capturable:\n            raise ValueError(\n                \"beta2 as a Tensor is not supported for capturable=False and foreach=True\"\n            )\n        if beta2.numel() != 1:\n            raise ValueError(\"Tensor beta2 must be 1-element\")\n\n    # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n        capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices(\n            supports_xla=False\n        )\n        assert all(\n            p.device.type == step.device.type\n            and p.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n            for p, step in zip(params, state_steps)\n        ), (\n            f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n        )\n\n    assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None\n\n    assert not differentiable, \"_foreach ops don't support autograd\"\n\n    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n    # TODO: Support nonzero-dim Tensor betas, see #147921\n\n    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        [params, grads, exp_avgs, exp_avg_sqs, max_exp_avg_sqs, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n    )\n\n    # We only shuffle around the beta when it is a Tensor and on CUDA, otherwise, we prefer\n    # treating it as a scalar.\n    beta1_dict: Optional[DeviceDict] = (  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n        {beta1.device: beta1}\n        if isinstance(beta1, Tensor) and str(beta1.device) != \"cpu\"\n        else None\n    )\n\n    for (\n        device_params_,\n        device_grads_,\n        device_exp_avgs_,\n        device_exp_avg_sqs_,\n        device_max_exp_avg_sqs_,\n        device_state_steps_,\n    ), _ in grouped_tensors.values():\n        device_params = cast(list[Tensor], device_params_)\n        device_grads = cast(list[Tensor], device_grads_)\n        device_exp_avgs = cast(list[Tensor], device_exp_avgs_)\n        device_exp_avg_sqs = cast(list[Tensor], device_exp_avg_sqs_)\n        device_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], device_state_steps_)\n\n        device = device_params[0].device\n        if beta1_dict is not None and device not in beta1_dict:\n            beta1_dict[device] = beta1.to(device=device, non_blocking=True)  # type: ignore[union-attr, attr-defined]\n\n        device_beta1 = beta1_dict[device] if beta1_dict else beta1\n\n        # Handle complex parameters\n        if has_complex:\n            if amsgrad:\n                device_max_exp_avg_sqs = cast(list[Tensor], device_max_exp_avg_sqs_)\n                _view_as_real(\n                    device_params,\n                    device_grads,\n                    device_exp_avgs,\n                    device_exp_avg_sqs,\n                    device_max_exp_avg_sqs,\n                )\n            else:\n                _view_as_real(\n                    device_params, device_grads, device_exp_avgs, device_exp_avg_sqs\n                )\n\n        if maximize:\n            device_grads = torch._foreach_neg(device_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n\n        # Update steps\n        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and device_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n            torch._foreach_add_(\n                device_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n            )\n        else:\n            torch._foreach_add_(device_state_steps, 1)\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            if decoupled_weight_decay:\n                # Perform stepweight decay\n                torch._foreach_mul_(device_params, 1 - lr * weight_decay)\n            else:\n                # Reuse the intermediate memory (device_grads) already allocated for maximize\n                if maximize:\n                    torch._foreach_add_(device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay)\n                else:\n                    device_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]\n                        device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay\n                    )\n\n        # Decay the first and second moment running average coefficient\n        # Use device beta1 if beta1 is a tensor to ensure all\n        # tensors are on the same device\n        torch._foreach_lerp_(\n            device_exp_avgs, device_grads, cast(float, 1 - device_beta1)\n        )\n\n        torch._foreach_mul_(device_exp_avg_sqs, beta2)\n\n        # Due to the strictness of the _foreach_addcmul API, we can't have a single\n        # tensor scalar as the scalar arg (only python number is supported there)\n        # as a result, separate out the value mul\n        # Filed https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/139795\n        if isinstance(beta2, torch.Tensor):\n            scaled_device_grads = torch._foreach_mul(device_grads, 1 - beta2)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n            value = 1.0\n        else:\n            scaled_device_grads = device_grads  # type: ignore[assignment]\n            value = 1 - beta2\n\n        torch._foreach_addcmul_(\n            device_exp_avg_sqs, scaled_device_grads, device_grads, value\n        )\n\n        # Delete the local intermediate(s) since they won't be used anymore to save on peak memory\n        del device_grads\n        del scaled_device_grads\n\n        bias_correction1: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n        bias_correction2: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n        bias_correction2_sqrt: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n\n        if capturable:\n            bias_correction1 = torch._foreach_pow(beta1, device_state_steps)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n            bias_correction2 = torch._foreach_pow(beta2, device_state_steps)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n            # foreach_sub doesn't allow a scalar as the first arg\n            torch._foreach_sub_(bias_correction1, 1)\n            torch._foreach_sub_(bias_correction2, 1)\n            # we do not negate bias_correction1 as it'll need to be negated later anyway\n            torch._foreach_neg_(bias_correction2)\n\n            # foreach_div doesn't allow a scalar as the first arg\n            torch._foreach_div_(bias_correction1, lr)\n            torch._foreach_reciprocal_(bias_correction1)\n\n            torch._foreach_sqrt_(bias_correction2)\n\n            # Re-assign for clarity as we maintain minimal intermediates: we'll have\n            # step_size = - lr / (1 - beta1 ^ t) where t = num_steps\n            # bias_correction2_sqrt = sqrt(1 - beta2 ^ t)\n            step_size = bias_correction1\n            bias_correction2_sqrt = bias_correction2\n\n            if amsgrad:\n                device_max_exp_avg_sqs = cast(list[Tensor], device_max_exp_avg_sqs_)\n                # Maintains the maximum of all 2nd moment running avg. till now\n                torch._foreach_maximum_(device_max_exp_avg_sqs, device_exp_avg_sqs)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n\n                # Set intermediate to the max. for normalizing running avg. of gradient when amsgrad\n                exp_avg_sq_sqrt = torch._foreach_sqrt(device_max_exp_avg_sqs)\n            else:\n                exp_avg_sq_sqrt = torch._foreach_sqrt(device_exp_avg_sqs)\n\n            torch._foreach_div_(exp_avg_sq_sqrt, bias_correction2_sqrt)\n            torch._foreach_add_(exp_avg_sq_sqrt, eps)\n            torch._foreach_div_(exp_avg_sq_sqrt, step_size)\n\n            # at this point, exp_avg_sq_sqrt = - (1 - beta^t) * [sqrt(exp_avg_sq / (1 - beta2^t)) + eps] / lr\n            torch._foreach_addcdiv_(device_params, device_exp_avgs, exp_avg_sq_sqrt)\n        else:\n            bias_correction1 = [\n                1 - beta1 ** _get_value(step) for step in device_state_steps\n            ]\n            bias_correction2 = [\n                1 - beta2 ** _get_value(step) for step in device_state_steps\n            ]\n\n            step_size = _stack_if_compiling([(lr / bc) * -1 for bc in bias_correction1])\n\n            bias_correction2_sqrt = [bc**0.5 for bc in bias_correction2]  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n\n            if amsgrad:\n                device_max_exp_avg_sqs = cast(list[Tensor], device_max_exp_avg_sqs_)\n                # Maintains the maximum of all 2nd moment running avg. till now\n                torch._foreach_maximum_(device_max_exp_avg_sqs, device_exp_avg_sqs)\n\n                # Use the max. for normalizing running avg. of gradient\n                exp_avg_sq_sqrt = torch._foreach_sqrt(device_max_exp_avg_sqs)\n            else:\n                exp_avg_sq_sqrt = torch._foreach_sqrt(device_exp_avg_sqs)\n\n            torch._foreach_div_(exp_avg_sq_sqrt, bias_correction2_sqrt)\n            torch._foreach_add_(exp_avg_sq_sqrt, eps)\n            torch._foreach_addcdiv_(\n                device_params,\n                device_exp_avgs,\n                exp_avg_sq_sqrt,\n                step_size,  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n            )\n\n\ndef _fused_adam(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    exp_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],\n    max_exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\n    *,\n    amsgrad: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,  # Needed for consistency.\n    beta1: float,\n    beta2: float,\n    lr: Union[float, Tensor],\n    weight_decay: float,\n    eps: float,\n    maximize: bool,\n    capturable: bool,  # Needed for consistency.\n    differentiable: bool,\n    decoupled_weight_decay: bool,\n) -> None:\n    if not params:\n        return\n    if differentiable:\n        raise RuntimeError(\"Adam with fused=True does not support differentiable=True\")\n\n    grad_scale_dict: DeviceDict = (\n        {grad_scale.device: grad_scale} if grad_scale is not None else {}\n    )\n    found_inf_dict: DeviceDict = (\n        {found_inf.device: found_inf} if found_inf is not None else {}\n    )\n\n    # type: ignore[list-item]\n    )\n    for (device, _), (\n        (\n            device_params_,\n            device_grads_,\n            device_exp_avgs_,\n            device_exp_avg_sqs_,\n            device_max_exp_avg_sqs,\n            device_state_steps_,\n        ),\n        _,\n    ) in grouped_tensors.items():\n        device_params = cast(list[Tensor], device_params_)\n        device_grads = cast(list[Tensor], device_grads_)\n        device_exp_avgs = cast(list[Tensor], device_exp_avgs_)\n        device_exp_avg_sqs = cast(list[Tensor], device_exp_avg_sqs_)\n        device_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], device_state_steps_)\n\n        device_grad_scale, device_found_inf = None, None\n        if grad_scale is not None:\n            device_grad_scale = grad_scale_dict.setdefault(\n                device, grad_scale.to(device, non_blocking=True)\n            )\n        if found_inf is not None:\n            device_found_inf = found_inf_dict.setdefault(\n                device, found_inf.to(device, non_blocking=True)\n            )\n        if lr_dict is not None and device not in lr_dict:\n            lr_dict[device] = lr.to(device=device, non_blocking=True)  # type: ignore[union-attr]\n            lr = lr_dict[device]\n        torch._foreach_add_(device_state_steps, 1)\n        func = torch._fused_adam_ if not decoupled_weight_decay else torch._fused_adamw_\n        func(\n            device_params,\n            device_grads,\n            device_exp_avgs,\n            device_exp_avg_sqs,\n            device_max_exp_avg_sqs,  # kwonly args with defaults are not supported by functions compiled with torchscript issue #70627\n    # setting this as kwarg for now as functional API is compiled by torch/distributed/optim\n    foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n    capturable: bool = False,\n    differentiable: bool = False,\n    fused: Optional[bool] = None,\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor] = None,\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor] = None,\n    has_complex: bool = False,\n    decoupled_weight_decay: bool = False,\n    *,\n    amsgrad: bool,\n    beta1: float,\n    beta2: float,\n    lr: Union[float, Tensor],\n    weight_decay: float,\n    eps: float,\n    maximize: bool,\n):\n    r\"\"\"Functional API that performs Adam algorithm computation.\n\n    See :class:`~torch.optim.Adam` for details.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Respect when the user inputs False/True for foreach or fused. We only want to change\n    # the default when neither have been user-specified. Note that we default to foreach\n    # and pass False to use_fused. This is not a mistake--we want to give the fused impl\n    # bake-in time before making it the default, even if it is typically faster.\n    if fused is None and foreach is None:\n        _, foreach = _default_to_fused_or_foreach(\n            params, differentiable, use_fused=False\n        )\n        # Do not flip on foreach for the unsupported case where lr is a Tensor and capturable=False.\n        if foreach and isinstance(lr, Tensor) and not capturable:\n            foreach = False\n    if fused is None:\n        fused = False\n    if foreach is None:\n        foreach = False\n\n    # this check is slow during compilation, so we skip it\n    # if it's strictly needed we can add this check back in dynamo\n    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and not all(\n        isinstance(t, torch.Tensor) for t in state_steps\n    ):\n        raise RuntimeError(\n            \"API has changed, `state_steps` argument must contain a list of singleton tensors\"\n        )\n\n    if foreach and torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        raise RuntimeError(\"torch.jit.script not supported with foreach optimizers\")\n    if fused and torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        raise RuntimeError(\"torch.jit.script not supported with fused optimizers\")\n\n    if fused and not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        func = _fused_adam\n    elif foreach and not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        func = _multi_tensor_adam\n    else:\n        func = _single_tensor_adam\n\n    func(\n        params,\n        grads,\n        exp_avgs,\n        exp_avg_sqs,\n        max_exp_avg_sqs,\n        state_steps,\n        amsgrad=amsgrad,\n        has_complex=has_complex,\n        beta1=beta1,\n        beta2=beta2,\n        lr=lr,\n        weight_decay=weight_decay,\n        eps=eps,\n        maximize=maximize,\n        capturable=capturable,\n        differentiable=differentiable,\n        grad_scale=grad_scale,\n        found_inf=found_inf,\n        decoupled_weight_decay=decoupled_weight_decay,\n    )\n","numLines":974,"startLine":1,"totalLines":974}}}

r\"\"\"Implements AdamW algorithm, where weight decay does not accumulate in the momentum nor variance.\n    63→\n    64→    .. math::\n    65→       \\begin{aligned}\n    66→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n    67→            &\\textbf{input}      : \\gamma \\text{(lr)}, \\: \\beta_1, \\beta_2\n    68→                \\text{(betas)}, \\: \\theta_0 \\text{(params)}, \\: f(\\theta) \\text{(objective)},\n    69→                \\: \\epsilon \\text{ (epsilon)}          \\\\\n    72→            &\\textbf{initialize} : m_0 \\leftarrow 0 \\text{ (first moment)}, v_0 \\leftarrow 0\n    73→                \\text{ ( second moment)}, \\: v_0^{max}\\leftarrow 0                        \\\\[-1.ex]\n    74→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n    75→            &\\textbf{for} \\: t=1 \\: \\begin{aligned}\n   183→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n   184→            &\\textbf{input}      : \\gamma \\text{ (lr)}, \\beta_1, \\beta_2\n   185→                \\text{ (betas)},\\theta_0 \\text{ (params)},f(\\theta) \\text{ (objective)},\n   186→                \\: \\lambda \\text{ (weight decay)},                                                \\\\\n   187→            &\\hspace{13mm}    \\epsilon \\text{ (epsilon)}                                          \\\\\n   188→            &\\textbf{initialize} :  m_0 \\leftarrow 0 \\text{ ( first moment)},\n   189→                u_0 \\leftarrow 0 \\text{ ( infinity norm)}                                 \\\\[-1.ex]\n   190→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n   191→            &\\textbf{for} \\: t=1 \\: \\textbf{to} \\: \\ldots \\: \\textbf{do}                         \\\\\n   192→            &\\hspace{5mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   \\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})           \\\\\n   193→            &\\hspace{5mm}if \\: \\lambda \\neq 0    &\\bf{return} \\:  \\theta_t                                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n   200→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n   201→       \\end{aligned}\n   202→\n   203→    For further details regarding the algorithm we refer to `Adam: A Method for Stochastic Optimization`_.\n   204→    \"\"\"\n   205→    + rf\"\"\"\n   206→    Args:\n   207→        {_params_doc}\n   208→        lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 2e-3)\n   209→        betas (Tuple[float, float], optional): coefficients 274→\n   275→        # Update biased first moment estimate.\n   276→        exp_avg.lerp_(grad, 1 - beta1)\n   277→        # Update the exponentially weighted infinity norm.\n   278→        if not # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n   368→        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is    params: list[Tensor],\n   199→    grads: list[Tensor],\n   200→    axs: list[Tensor],\n   201→    mus: list[Tensor],\n   202→    etas: list[Tensor],\n   203→    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n   204→  212→    capturable: bool,\n   213→    has_complex: bool,\n   214→):\n   215→    if not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n   216→        lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n   217→\n   218→    for i, param in        step_t = state_steps[i]\n   225→\n   226→        # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n   227→        if not torch.view_as_real(param)\n   243→            ax = torch.view_as_real(ax)\n   244→\n   245→        # update step\n   246→        step_t += 1\n   247→\n   248→        if weight_decay != 0:\n   249→            grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\n   250→\n   251→        if capturable:\n   252→            param.mul_(1 - lambd * eta)\n   253→            param.addcmul_(grad, eta, value=-1)  # update parameter\n   254→        else:\n   255→            eta_value = _get_value(eta)\n   256→            param.mul_(1 - lambd * eta_value)  # decay term\n   257→            param.add_(grad, alpha=-eta_value)  # update parameter\n   258→\n   259→        # averaging\n   260→        if capturable or mu.item() != 1:\n   261→            ax.add_(param.sub(ax).mul_(mu))\n   262→        else:\n   263→            ax.copy_(param)\n   264→\n   265→        if capturable:\n   266→            eta.copy_(lr / ((1 + lambd * lr * step_t) ** alpha))\n   267→            mu.copy_(1 / torch.maximum(step_t - t0, torch.ones_like(step_t)))\n   268→        else:\n   269→            step = _get_value(step_t)\n   270→            new_eta = torch.as_tensor(lr / ((1 + lambd * lr * step) ** alpha))\n   271→            eta.copy_(new_eta)\n   272→            new_mu = torch.as_tensor(1 / max(1, step - t0))\n   273→            mu.copy_(new_mu)\n   274→\n   275→\n   276→def _multi_tensor_asgd(\n   277→    params: list[Tensor],\n   278→    grads: list[Tensor],\n   279→    axs: list[Tensor],\n   280→    mus: list[Tensor],\n   281→    etas: list[Tensor],\n   282→    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n   283→    *,\n capturable: bool,\n   292→    has_complex: bool,\n   293→):\n   294→    if len(params) == 0:\n   295→        return\n   296→\n   297→    assert not differentiable, \"_foreach ops don't support autograd\"\n   298→\n   299→    # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n   300→    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n   301→        capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices(\n   302→            supports_xla=False\n   303→        )\n   304→        assert all(\n   305→            p.device.type == grouped_mus = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_mus_)\n   332→        grouped_etas = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_etas_)\n   333→        grouped_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_state_steps_)\n   334→\n   335→        if has_complex:\n   336→            _view_as_real(grouped_params, grouped_grads, grouped_axs)\n   337→\n   338→        if maximize:\n   339→            grouped_grads = torch._foreach_neg(grouped_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n   340→\n   341→        # Update steps\n   342→        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n   343→        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n   344→        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n   345→        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and grouped_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n   346→            torch._foreach_add_(\n   347→                grouped_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n   348→            )\n   349→        else:\n   350→            torch._foreach_add_(grouped_state_steps, 1)\n   351→\n   352→        # intermediate = grad + param * lambd\n   353→        intermediate: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n   354→        if weight_decay != 0:\n   355→            if maximize:\n   356→                torch._foreach_add_(grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay)\n   357→                intermediate = grouped_grads\n   358→            else:\n   359→                intermediate = torch._foreach_add(\n   360→                    grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay\n   361→                )\n   362→\n   363→            torch._foreach_add_(intermediate, grouped_params, alpha=lambd)\n   364→        else:\n   365→            intermediate = torch._foreach_add(\n   366→                grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=lambd\n   367→            )\n   368→\n   369→        # update param\n   370→        # param * (1 - lambd * eta) - eta * grad\n   371→        # => param - param * lambd * eta - eta * grad\n   372→        # => param - eta * intermediate\n   373→        torch._foreach_addcmul_(grouped_params, intermediate, grouped_etas, value=-1)\n   374→        del intermediate\n   375→\n   376→        # update grouped_axs\n   377→        # averaging: ax = ax + mu * (param - ax)\n   378→        # Note (mlazos): We can't use lerp here since it requires weight to be float64\n   379→        # and our grouping code requires dtypes to match for all tensors in a group (and it should, since\n   380→        # we use the mus in other places)\n   381→        # all dtypes need to match, so we could introduce a cast in a loop\n   382→        # but since this only adds one additional kernel launch, this looks like the cleaner\n   383→        # and faster solution\n   384→        intermediate = torch._foreach_sub(grouped_params, grouped_axs)\n   385→        torch._foreach_addcmul_(grouped_axs, intermediate, grouped_mus)\n   386→        del intermediate\n   387→\n   388→        new_etas: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n   389→        new_mus: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n   390→        if capturable:\n   391→            # update eta = lr / ((1 + lambd * lr * step)^alpha)\n   399→            new_etas = torch._foreach_mul(grouped_state_steps, lambd)\n   400→            torch._foreach_mul_(new_etas, lr)\n   401→            torch._foreach_add_(new_etas, 1)\n   402→            torch._foreach_pow_(new_etas, alpha)\n   403→            torch._foreach_reciprocal_(new_etas)\n   404→            torch._foreach_mul_(new_etas, lr)\n   405→            torch._foreach_copy_(grouped_etas, new_etas)\n   406→        else:\n   407→            new_etas = [\n   408→                torch.as_tensor(lr / ((1 + lambd * lr * step) ** alpha), device=device)\n   409→                for step in grouped_state_steps\n   410→            ]\n   411→            new_mus = [\n   412→                torch.as_tensor(1 / max(1, _get_value(step) - t0), device=device)\n   413→                for step in grouped_state_steps\n   414→            ]\n   415→            torch._foreach_copy_(grouped_etas, new_etas)\n   416→            torch._foreach_copy_(grouped_mus, new_mus)\n   417→\n   418→\n   419→@_disable_dynamo_if_unsupported(single_tensor_fn=_single_tensor_asgd)\n   420→def asgd(\n   421→    params: list[Tensor],\n   422→    grads: list[Tensor],\n   423→    axs: list[Tensor],\n   424→    mus: list[Tensor],\n   425→    etas: list[Tensor],\n   426→    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n   427→    # 181→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n   182→            &\\textbf{input}      : \\gamma \\text{ (lr)}, \\: \\beta_1, \\beta_2\n   183→                \\text{ (betas)}, \\: \\theta_0 \\text{ (params)}, \\:f(\\theta) \\text{ (objective)}, \\:\n   184→                \\lambda \\text{ (weightdecay)}, \\:\\textit{maximize}                               \\\\\n   185→            &\\hspace{13mm} \\epsilon \\text{ (epsilon)}, \\textit{decoupled\\_weight\\_decay}         \\\\\n   186→            &\\textbf{initialize} :  m_0 \\leftarrow 0 \\text{ ( first moment)},\n   187→                v_0 \\leftarrow 0 \\text{ ( second moment)},                                       \\\\\n   188→            &\\hspace{18mm} \\rho_{\\infty} \\leftarrow 2/(1-\\beta_2) -1                      \\\\[-1.ex]\n   189→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}  \\\\\n   190→            &\\textbf{for} \\: t=1 \\: \\textbf{to} \\: \\ldots \\: \\textbf{do}                         \\\\\n   191→            &\\hspace{6mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\textit{maximize}:                                       \\\\\n   192→            &\\hspace{12mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   -\\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})         \\\\\n   193→            &\\hspace{6mm}\\textbf{else}                                                           \\\\\n   194→            &\\hspace{12mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   \\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})          \\\\\n   195→            &\\hspace{6mm} \\theta_t \\leftarrow \\theta_{t-1}                                       \\\\\n   196→            &\\hspace{6mm} \\textbf{if} \\: \\lambda \\neq 0                                          \\\\\n   197→            &\\hspace{12mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\textit{decoupled\\_weight\\_decay}                       \\\\\n   198→            &\\hspace{18mm} \\theta_t \\leftarrow \\theta_{t} - \\gamma \\lambda \\theta_{t}            \\\\\n   199→            &\\hspace{12mm}\\textbf{else}                                                          \\\\\n   200→            &\\hspace{18mm} g_t \\leftarrow g_t + \\lambda \\theta_{t}                               \\\\\n   201→            &\\hspace{6mm}m_t           \\leftarrow   \\beta_1 m_{t-1} + (1 - \\beta_1) g_t          \\\\\n   202→            &\\hspace{6mm}v_t           \\leftarrow   \\beta_2 v_{t-1} + (1-\\beta_2) g^2_t          \\\\\n   203→            &\\hspace{6mm}\\widehat{m_t} \\leftarrow   m_t/\\big(1-\\beta_1^t \\big)                   \\\\\n   204→            &\\hspace{6mm}\\rho_t \\leftarrow \\rho_{\\infty} -\n   205→                2 t \\beta^t_2 /\\big(1-\\beta_2^t \\big)                                    \\\\[0.1.ex]\n   206→            &\\hspace{6mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\rho_t > 5                                               \\\\\n   207→            &\\hspace{12mm} l_t \\leftarrow \\frac{\\sqrt{ (1-\\beta^t_2) }}{ \\sqrt{v_t} +\\epsilon  } \\\\\n   208→            &\\hspace{12mm} r_t \\leftarrow\n   209→      \\sqrt{\\frac{(\\rho_t-4)(\\rho_t-2)\\rho_{\\infty}}{(\\rho_{\\infty}-4)(\\rho_{\\infty}-2) \\rho_t}} \\\\\n   210→            &\\hspace{12mm}\\theta_t \\leftarrow \\theta_t - \\gamma \\widehat{m_t} r_t l_t        \\\\\n   211→            &\\hspace{6mm}\\textbf{else}                                                           \\\\\n   212→            &\\hspace{12mm}\\theta_t \\leftarrow \\theta_t - \\gamma \\widehat{m_t}                \\\\\n   213→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n   214→            &\\bf{return} \\:  \\theta_t                                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n   215→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n   216→       \\end{aligned}\n   217→\n   218→    For further details regarding the algorithm we refer to `On the variance of the adaptive learning rate and beyond`_.\n   219→\n   220→    This implementation provides an option to use either the original weight_decay implementation as in Adam\n   221→    (where the weight_decay is applied to the gradient) or the one from AdamW (where weight_decay is applied\n   222→    to the weight) through the decoupled_weight_decay option. When decoupled_weight_decay is set to False\n   223→    (default), it uses the original Adam style weight decay, otherwise, it uses the AdamW style which\n   224→    corresponds more closely to the `author's implementation`_ in the RAdam paper. Further information\n   225→    about decoupled weight decay can be found in `Decoupled Weight Decay Regularization`_.\n   226→\n   227→    \"\"\"\n   228→    + rf\"\"\"\n   229→    Args:\n   230→        {_params_doc}\n   231→        lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 1e-3)\n   232→        betas (Tuple[float, float], optional): coefficients used for computing\n   233→            running averages of gradient and its square (default: (0.9, 0.999))\n   234→        eps (float, optional): term added to the denominator to improve\n   235→            numerical stability (default: 1e-8)\n   236→        weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay (L2 penalty) (default: 0)\n   237→        decoupled_weight_decay (bool, optional): whether to decouple the weight\n   238→            decay as in AdamW to obtain RAdamW. If True, the algorithm does not\n   239→            accumulate weight decay in the momentum nor variance. (default: False)\n   240→        {_foreach_doc}\n   241→        {_maximize_doc}\n   242→        {_capturable_doc}\n   243→        {_differentiable_doc}\n   244→\n   245→    .. _On the variance of the adaptive learning rate and beyond:\n   246→        https://arxiv.org/abs/1908.03265\n   247→    .. _author's implementation:\n   248→        https://github.com/LiyuanLucasLiu/RAdam\n   249→    .. _Decoupled Weight Decay Regularization:\n   250→        https://arxiv.org/abs/1711.05101\n   251→\n   252→    \"\"\"\n   253→)\n   254→\n   255→\n   256→def _single_tensor_radam(\n   257→    params: list[Tensor],\n   258→    grads: list[Tensor],\n   259→    exp_avgs: list[Tensor],\n   260→    exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],\n   261→    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n   262→    *,\n   263→    beta1: float,\n   264→    beta2: float,\n   265→    lr: float,\n   266→    weight_decay: float,\n   267→    eps: float,\n   268→    decoupled_weight_decay: bool,\n   269→    differentiable: bool,\n   270→    maximize: bool,\n   271→    capturable: bool,\n   272→    has_complex: bool,\n   273→):\n   274→    if not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n   275→        lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n   276→\n   277→    for i, param in enumerate(params):\n   278→        grad = grads[i] if not maximize else -grads[i]\n   279→        exp_avg = exp_avgs[i]\n   280→        exp_avg_sq = exp_avg_sqs[i]\n   281→        step_t = state_steps[i]\n   282→\n   283→        # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n   284→        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n   285→            capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices()\n   286→            assert (\n   287→                param.device.type == step_t.device.type\n   288→                and param.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n   289→            ), (\n   290→                f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n   291→            )\n   292→\n   293→        if torch.is_complex(param):\n   294→            param = torch.view_as_real(param)\n   295→            grad = torch.view_as_real(grad)\n   296→            exp_avg = torch.view_as_real(exp_avg)\n   297→            exp_avg_sq = torch.view_as_real(exp_avg_sq)\n   298→\n   299→        # update step\n   300→        step_t += 1\n   301→        step = step_t if capturable else _get_value(step_t)\n   302→\n   303→        if weight_decay != 0:\n   304→            if decoupled_weight_decay:\n   305→                param.mul_(1 - lr * weight_decay)\n   306→            else:\n   307→                grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\n   308→\n   309→        # Decay the first and second moment running average coefficient\n   310→        exp_avg.lerp_(grad, 1 - beta1)\n   311→        exp_avg_sq.mul_(beta2).addcmul_(grad, grad, value=1 - beta2)\n   312→\n   313→        bias_correction1 = 1 - beta1**step\n   314→        bias_correction2 = 1 - beta2**step\n   315→\n   316→        # correcting bias for the first moving moment\n   317→        bias_corrected_exp_avg = exp_avg / bias_correction1\n   318→\n   319→        # maximum length of the approximated SMA\n   320→        rho_inf = 2 / (1 - beta2) - 1\n   321→        # compute the length of the approximated SMA\n   322→        rho_t = rho_inf - 2 * step * (beta2**step) / bias_correction2\n   323→\n   324→        def _compute_rect():\n   325→            return (\n   326→                (rho_t - 4)\n   327→                * (rho_t - 2)\n   328→                * rho_inf\n   329→                / ((rho_inf - 4) * (rho_inf - 2) * rho_t)\n   330→            ) ** 0.5\n   331→\n   332→        def _compute_adaptive_lr():\n   333→            exp_avg_sq_sqrt = exp_avg_sq.sqrt()\n   334→            if differentiable:\n   335→                exp_avg_sq_sqrt = exp_avg_sq_sqrt.add(eps)\n   336→            else:\n   337→                exp_avg_sq_sqrt = exp_avg_sq_sqrt.add_(eps)\n   338→\n   339→            return (bias_correction2**0.5) / exp_avg_sq_sqrt\n   340→\n   341→        # Compute the variance rectification term and update parameters accordingly\n   342→        if capturable:\n   343→            update = torch.where(\n   344→                rho_t > 5.0, _compute_rect() * _compute_adaptive_lr(), 1.0\n   345→            )\n   346→            param.add_(bias_corrected_exp_avg * lr * update, alpha=-1.0)\n   347→        else:\n   348→            if rho_t > 5.0:\n   349→                param.add_(\n   350→                    bias_corrected_exp_avg\n   351→                    * lr\n   352→                    * _compute_adaptive_lr()\n   353→                    * _compute_rect(),\n   354→                    alpha=-1.0,\n   355→                )\n   356→            else:\n   357→                param.add_(bias_corrected_exp_avg * lr, alpha=-1.0)\n   358→\n   359→\n   360→def _multi_tensor_radam(\n   361→    params: list[Tensor],\n   362→    grads: list[Tensor],\n   363→    exp_avgs: list[Tensor],\n   364→    exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],\n   365→    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n   366→    *,\n   367→    beta1: float,\n   368→    beta2: float,\n   369→    lr: float,\n   370→    weight_decay: float,\n   371→    eps: float,\n   372→    decoupled_weight_decay: bool,\n   373→    differentiable: bool,\n   374→    maximize: bool,\n   375→    capturable: bool,\n   376→    has_complex: bool,\n   377→):\n   378→    if len(params) == 0:\n   379→        return\n   380→\n   381→    assert not differentiable, \"_foreach ops don't support autograd\"\n   382→\n   383→    # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n   384→    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n   385→        capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices(\n   386→            supports_xla=False\n   387→        )\n   388→        assert all(\n   389→            p.device.type == step.device.type\n   390→            and p.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n   391→            for p, step in zip(params, state_steps)\n   392→        ), (\n   393→            f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n   394→        )\n   395→\n   396→    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n   397→\n   398→    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n   399→        [params, grads, exp_avgs, exp_avg_sqs, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n   400→    )\n   401→    for (\n   402→        grouped_params_,\n   403→        grouped_grads_,\n   404→        grouped_exp_avgs_,\n   405→        grouped_exp_avg_sqs_,\n   406→        grouped_state_steps_,\n   407→    ), _ in grouped_tensors.values():\n   408→        grouped_params = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_params_)\n   409→        grouped_grads = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_grads_)\n   410→        grouped_exp_avgs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_exp_avgs_)\n   411→        grouped_exp_avg_sqs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_exp_avg_sqs_)\n   412→        grouped_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_state_steps_)\n   413→\n   414→        # Update steps\n   415→        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n   416→        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n   417→        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n   418→        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and grouped_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n   419→            torch._foreach_add_(\n   420→                grouped_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n   421→            )\n   422→        else:\n   423→            torch._foreach_add_(grouped_state_steps, 1)\n   424→\n   425→        if has_complex:\n   426→            _view_as_real(\n   427→                grouped_params, grouped_grads, grouped_exp_avgs, grouped_exp_avg_sqs\n   428→            )\n   429→\n   430→        if maximize:\n   431→            grouped_grads = torch._foreach_neg(grouped_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n   432→\n   433→        # maximum length of the approximated SMA\n   434→        rho_inf = 2 / (1 - beta2) - 1\n   435→        # compute the length of the approximated SMA\n   436→        bias_correction1: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n   437→        bias_correction2: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n   438→        rho_t_list: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n   439→        if capturable:\n   440→            bias_correction1 = torch._foreach_pow(beta2, grouped_state_steps)\n   441→            torch._foreach_neg_(bias_correction1)\n   442→            torch._foreach_add_(bias_correction1, 1)\n   443→            bias_correction2 = torch._foreach_pow(beta2, grouped_state_steps)\n   444→            torch._foreach_mul_(bias_correction2, grouped_state_steps)\n   445→ torch._foreach_mul_(unrect_step_size, lr)\n   505→\n   506→            bias_correction1 = torch._foreach_pow(beta1, grouped_state_steps)\n   507→            torch._foreach_neg_(bias_correction1)\n   531→                ** 0.5\n   532→                if rho_t > 5\n   533→                else 0\n   534→                for rho_t in rho_t_list\n   535→            ]\n   536→            unrectified = [0 if rect > 0 else 1.0 for rect in rect]\n   537→\n   538→            bias_correction1 = [\n   539→                1 - beta1 ** _get_value(step) for step in grouped_state_steps\n   540→            ]\n   541→            unrect_step_size = [\n   542→                (lr * rect / bc) * -1 for rect, bc in zip(unrectified, bias_correction1)\n   543→            ]\n   544→            bias_correction2 =      ]\n   548→\n   549→        buffer = torch._foreach_sqrt(grouped_exp_avg_sqs)\n   550→        torch._foreach_add_(buffer, eps)\n   551→        torch._foreach_div_(buffer, bias_correction2)\n   552→        torch._foreach_reciprocal_(buffer)\n   553→        torch._foreach_add_(buffer, unrect_step_size)\n   554→\n   555→        # Here, buffer = sqrt(1 - beta2^t) * rect_step_size / (sqrt(v) + eps) + unrect_step_size\n   556→        torch._foreach_addcmul_(grouped_params, grouped_exp_avgs, buffer)\n   557→\n   558→\n _init_group(\n    94→        self,\n    95→        group,\n    96→        params_with_grad,\n    97→        grads,\n    98→        square_avgs,\n    99→        momentum_buffer_list,\n   100→        grad_avgs,\n   101→        state_steps,\n   102→    ):\n   103→        has_complex = False\n   104→        for p in group[\"params\"]:\n   105→            if p.grad is None:\n   106→               if group[\"capturable\"]\n   121→                    else torch.zeros((), dtype=_get_scalar_dtype())\n   122→                )\n   123→                state[\"square_avg\"] = 131→                    state[\"grad_avg\"] = torch.zeros_like(\n   132→                        p, memory_format=torch.preserve_format\n   133→                    )\n   134→ momentum_buffer_list.append(state[\"momentum_buffer\"])\n   139→            if group[\"centered\"]:\n   140→                grad_avgs.append(state[\"grad_avg\"])\n   141→\n   142→        return has_complex\n   143→\n   144→    @_use_grad_for_differentiable\n   145→    def step(self, closure=None):\n   146→        \"\"\"Perform a single optimization step.\n   147→\n   148→        Args:\n   149→            closure (Callable, optional): A closure that reevaluates the model\n   150→                and returns the loss.\n   151→        \"\"\"\n   152→        self._cuda_graph_capture_health_check()\n   153→\n   154→        loss = None\n   155→        if closure is not None:\n   156→            with torch.enable_grad():\n   157→                loss = 197→        return loss\n   198→\n   199→\n   200→RMSprop.__doc__ = (\n   201→    r\"\"\"Implements RMSprop algorithm.\n   202→\n   203→    .. math::\n   204→       \\begin{aligned}\n   205→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n   206→            &\\textbf{input}      : \\alpha \\text{ (alpha)}, \\: \\gamma \\text{ (lr)},\n   207→                \\: \\theta_0 \\text{ (params)}, \\: f(\\theta) \\text{ (objective)}                   \\\\\n   208→            &\\hspace{13mm}   \\lambda \\text{ (weight decay)},\\: \\mu \\text{ (momentum)},\n   209→                \\: centered, \\: \\epsilon \\text{ (epsilon)}                                       \\\\\n   210→            &\\textbf{initialize} : v_0 \\leftarrow 0 \\text{ (square average)}, \\:\n   211→                \\textbf{b}_0 \\leftarrow 0 \\text{ (buffer)}, \\: g^{ave}_0 \\leftarrow 0     \\\\[-1.ex]\n   212→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n   213→            &\\textbf{for} \\: t=1 \\: \\textbf{to} \\: \\ldots \\: \\textbf{do}                         \\\\\n   214→            &\\hspace{5mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   \\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})           \\\\\n   215→            &\\hspace{5mm}if \\: \\lambda \\neq 0                                                    \\\\\n   216→            &\\hspace{10mm} g_t \\leftarrow g_t + \\lambda  \\theta_{t-1}                            \\\\\n   217→            &\\hspace{5mm}v_t           \\leftarrow   \\alpha v_{t-1} + (1 - \\alpha) g^2_t\n   218→                \\hspace{8mm}                                                                     \\\\\n   219→            &\\hspace{5mm} \\tilde{v_t} \\leftarrow v_t                                             \\\\\n   220→            &\\hspace{5mm}if \\: centered                                                          \\\\\n   221→            &\\hspace{10mm} g^{ave}_t \\leftarrow g^{ave}_{t-1} \\alpha + (1-\\alpha) g_t            \\\\\n   222→            &\\hspace{10mm} \\tilde{v_t} \\leftarrow \\tilde{v_t} -  \\big(g^{ave}_{t} \\big)^2        \\\\\n   223→            &\\hspace{5mm}if \\: \\mu > 0                                                           \\\\\n   224→            &\\hspace{10mm} \\textbf{b}_t\\leftarrow \\mu \\textbf{b}_{t-1} +\n   225→                g_t/ \\big(\\sqrt{\\tilde{v_t}} +  \\epsilon \\big)                                   \\\\\n   226→            &\\hspace{10mm} \\theta_t \\leftarrow \\theta_{t-1} - \\gamma \\textbf{b}_t                \\\\\n   227→            &\\hspace{5mm} else                                                                   \\\\\n   228→            &\\hspace{10mm}\\theta_t      \\leftarrow   \\theta_{t-1} -\n   229→                \\gamma  g_t/ \\big(\\sqrt{\\tilde{v_t}} + \\epsilon \\big)  \\hspace{3mm}              \\\\\n   230→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n   231→            &\\bf{return} \\:  \\theta_t                                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n   232→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n   233→       \\end{aligned}\n   234→\n   235→    For further details regarding the algorithm we refer to\n   236→    `lecture notes <https://www.cs.toronto.edu/~tijmen/csc321/slides/lecture_slides_lec6.pdf>`_ by G. Hinton.\n   237→    and centered version `Generating Sequences\n   238→    With Recurrent Neural Networks <https://arxiv.org/pdf/1308.0850v5.pdf>`_.\n   239→    The implementation here takes the square root of the gradient average before\n   240→    adding epsilon (note that TensorFlow interchanges these two operations). The effective\n   241→    learning rate is thus :math:`\\gamma/(\\sqrt{v} + \\epsilon)` where :math:`\\gamma`\n   242→    is the scheduled learning rate and :math:`v` is the weighted moving average\n   243→    of the squared gradient.\n   244→    \"\"\"\n   245→    + rf\"\"\"\n   246→    Args:\n   247→        {_params_doc}\n   248→        lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 1e-2)\n   249→        alpha (float, optional): smoothing constant (default: 0.99)\n   250→        eps (float, optional): term added to the denominator to improve\n   251→            numerical stability (default: 1e-8)\n   252→        weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay (L2 penalty) (default: 0)\n   253→        momentum (float, optional): momentum factor (default: 0)\n   254→  {_foreach_doc}\n   258→        {_maximize_doc}\n   259→        {_differentiable_doc}\n   260→\n   261→    \"\"\"\n   262→)\n   263→\n   264→\n   265→def _single_tensor_rmsprop(\n   266→    params: list[Tensor],\n   272→    *,\n   273→    lr: float,\n   274→    alpha: float,\n   275→    eps: float,\n   276→    weight_decay: float,\n   277→    momentum: float,\n   278→    centered: bool,\n   279→    maximize: bool,\n   280→    differentiable: bool,\n   281→    capturable: bool,\n   282→    has_complex: bool,\n   283→):\n   284→    if not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n   285→        lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n   286→\n   287→    for i, param in enumerate(params):\n   288→        step = state_steps[i]\n   289→\n   290→        # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see          assert (\n   294→                param.device.type == step.device.type\n   295→                and param.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n   296→            ), (\n   297→                f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n   298→            )\n   299→\n   300→        grad = grads[i]\n   301→        grad grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\n   308→\n   309→        is_complex_param = torch.is_complex(param)\n   310→        if is_complex_param:\n   311→            param = torch.view_as_real(param)\n   312→            grad = torch.view_as_real(grad)\n   313→            square_avg = torch.view_as_real(square_avg)\n   314→\n   315→        square_avg.mul_(alpha).addcmul_(grad, grad, value=1 - alpha)\n   316→\n   317→        if centered:\n   318→            grad_avg = grad_avgs[i]\n   319→            if is_complex_param:\n   320→                grad_avg = torch.view_as_real(grad_avg)\n   321→            grad_avg.lerp_(grad, 1 - alpha)\n   322→            avg = square_avg.addcmul(grad_avg, grad_avg, value=-1).sqrt_()\n   323→        else:\n   324→            avg = square_avg.sqrt()\n   325→\n   326→        if differentiable:\n   327→            avg = avg.add(eps)\n   328→        else:\n   329→            avg = avg.add_(eps)\n   330→\n   331→        if momentum > 0:\n   332→            buf = momentum_buffer_list[i]\n   333→            if is_complex_param:\n   334→                buf = torch.view_as_real(buf)\n   335→            buf.mul_(momentum).addcdiv_(grad, avg)\n   336→            param.add_(buf, alpha=-lr)\n   337→        else:\n   338→            param.addcdiv_(grad, avg, value=-lr)\n   339→\n   340→\n   341→def _multi_tensor_rmsprop(\n   342→    params: list[Tensor],\n _init_group(self, group, params, grads, prevs, step_sizes, state_steps):\n    81→        has_complex = False\n    82→        for p in group[\"params\"]:\n    83→            if p.grad is None:\n    84→                continue\n    85→            has_complex |= torch.is_complex(p)\n    86→            params.append(p)\n    87→            grad = p.grad\n    88→            if grad.is_sparse:\n    89→                raise RuntimeError(\"Rprop does not support sparse gradients\")\n    90→\n    91→            grads.append(grad)\n    92→            state = self.state[p]\n    93→\n    94→            # 98→                    if group[\"capturable\"]\n    99→                    else torch.zeros((), dtype=_get_scalar_dtype())\n   100→                )\n   101→\n   102→                state[\"prev\"] = torch.zeros_like(p, memory_format=torch.preserve_format)\n   103→                if p.dtype.is_complex:\n   104→                    # Complex Number should be as if they are two independent real numbers.\n   105→                    # Hence the step_size shouldn't be zero for imaginary part.\n   106→                    state[\"step_size\"] = torch.full_like(\n   107→                        grad, complex(group[\"lr\"], group[\"lr\"])\n   108→                    )\n   109→                else:\n   110→                    state[\"step_size\"] = torch.full_like(grad, \"\"\"Perform a single optimization step.\n   121→\n   122→        Args:\n   123→            closure (Callable, optional): A closure that reevaluates the model\n   124→                and returns the loss.\n   125→        \"\"\"\n   126→        self._cuda_graph_capture_health_check()\n   127→\n   128→        loss = None\n   129→        if closure is not None:\n   130→            with                        \\\\[-1.ex]\n   199→       \\end{aligned}\n   200→\n   201→    For further details regarding the algorithm we refer to the paper\n   202→    `A Direct Adaptive Method for Faster # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n   247→        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n   248→ 260→            prev = torch.view_as_real(prev)\n   261→            param = torch.view_as_real(param)\n   262→            step_size = torch.view_as_real(step_size)\n   263→        if differentiable:\n   264→            sign = grad.mul(prev.clone()).sign()\n   265→        else:\n   266→            sign = grad.mul(prev).sign()\n   267→\n   268→        if capturable:\n   269→    else:\n   273→            sign[sign.gt(0)] = etaplus\n   274→            sign[sign.lt(0)] = etaminus\n   275→            sign[sign.eq(0)] = 1\n   276→\n   277→        # update stepsizes with step 339→        grouped_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_state_steps_)\n   340→\n   341→        # Update steps\n   342→        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n   343→        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n   344→        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n   345→        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and grouped_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n   346→            torch._foreach_add_(\n   347→                grouped_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n   348→            )\n   349→        else:\n   350→            torch._foreach_add_(grouped_state_steps, 1)\n   351→\n   352→        # \\begin{aligned}\n   158→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n   159→            &\\textbf{input}      : \\gamma \\text{ (lr)}, \\: \\theta_0 \\text{ (params)}, \\: f(\\theta)\n   160→                \\text{ (objective)}, \\: \\lambda \\text{ (weight decay)},                          \\\\\n   161→            &\\hspace{13mm} &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n   164→            &\\textbf{for} \\: t=1 \\: \\textbf{to} \\: \\ldots \\: \\textbf{do}                         \\\\\n   165→            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\textit{maximize}:                                       \\\\\n   166→            &\\hspace{10mm}g_t           \\leftarrow &\\hspace{15mm} \\textbf{b}_t \\leftarrow g_t                                           \\\\\n   176→            &\\hspace{10mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\textit{nesterov} \\theta_{t-1} - \\gamma g_t                    \\\\[-1.ex]\n   181→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n   182→            &\\bf{return} \\:  \\theta_t                                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n   183→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n   184→       \\end{aligned}\n   185→\n   186→    Nesterov momentum is based on the formula from\n   187→    `On the importance of initialization and momentum in deep learning`__.\n   188→    \"\"\"\n   189→    + rf\"\"\"\n   190→    Args:\n   191→        {_params_doc}\n   192→        lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 1e-3)\n   193→        momentum (float, (default: 0)\n   196→        nesterov (bool, optional): enables Nesterov momentum. Only applicable\n   197→            when momentum is non-zero. (default: False)\n   198→        {_maximize_doc}\n   199→        {_foreach_doc}\n   200→        {_differentiable_doc}\n   201→        {_fused_doc}\n   202→    \"\"\"\n   203→    + r\"\"\"\n   204→\n   205→    Example:\n   206→        >>> # xdoctest: +SKIP\n   207→        >>> optimizer = torch.optim.SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.1, momentum=0.9)\n   208→        >>> optimizer.zero_grad()\n   209→        >>> loss_fn(model(input), target).backward()\n   210→        >>> optimizer.step()\n   211→\n   212→    __ http://www.cs.toronto.edu/%7Ehinton/absps/momentum.pdf\n   213→\n   214→    .. note::\n   215→        The implementation of SGD with Momentum/Nesterov subtly differs from\n   216→        Sutskever et al. and implementations in some other frameworks.\n   217→\n   218→        Considering the specific case of Momentum, the update can be written as\n   219→\n   220→        .. math::\n   221→            \\begin{aligned}\n   222→                v_{t+1} & = \\mu * v_{t} + g_{t+1}, \\\\\n   223→                p_{t+1} & = p_{t} - \\text{lr} * v_{t+1},\n   224→            \\end{aligned}\n   225→\n   226→        where :math:`p`, :math:`g`, :math:`v` and :math:`\\mu` denote the\n   227→        parameters, gradient, velocity, and momentum respectively.\n   228→\n   229→        This is in contrast to Sutskever et al. and\n   230→        other frameworks which employ an update of the form\n   231→\n   232→        .. math::\n   233→            \\begin{aligned}\n   234→                v_{t+1} & = \\mu * v_{t} + \\text{lr} * g_{t+1}, \\\\\n   235→                p_{t+1} & = p_{t} - v_{t+1}.\n   236→            \\end{aligned}\n   237→\n   238→        The Nesterov version is analogously modified.\n   239→\n   240→        Moreover, the initial value of the momentum buffer is set to the\n   241→        gradient value at the first step. This is in contrast to some other\n   242→        frameworks that initialize it to all zeros. One notable side effect\n   243→        of this decision is that the first momentum value will not be scaled\n   244→        by dampening. Dampening will be applied starting at the second step.\n   245→\n   246→    \"\"\"\n   247→)\n   248→\n   249→\n   250→def sgd(\n   251→    params: list[Tensor],\n   252→    d_p_list: list[Tensor],\n   253→    momentum_buffer_list: list[Optional[Tensor]],\n   254→    # kwonly args with defaults are not supported by functions compiled with torchscript issue #70627\n   255→    # setting this as kwarg for now as functional API is compiled by torch/distributed/optim\n   256→    has_sparse_grad: bool = False,\n   257→    foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n   258→    fused: Optional[bool] = None,\n   259→    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor] = None,\n   260→    found_inf: Optional[Tensor] = None,\n   261→    *,\n   262→    weight_decay: float,\n   263→    momentum: float,\n   264→    lr: float,\n   265→    dampening: float,\n   266→    nesterov: bool,\n   267→    maximize: bool,\n   268→):\n   269→    r\"\"\"Functional API that performs SGD algorithm computation.\n   270→\n   271→    See :class:`~torch.optim.SGD` for details.\n   272→    \"\"\"\n   273→    # Respect when the user inputs False/True for foreach or fused.
