group.setdefault(\"maximize\", False)\n   119→            group.setdefault(\"foreach\", None)\n   120→            group.setdefault(\"capturable\", False)\n   121→            group.setdefault(\"differentiable\", False)\n   122→            group.setdefault(\"decoupled_weight_decay\", False)\n   123→            fused = group.setdefault(\"fused\", None)\n   124→            for p in group[\"params\"]:\n   125→                p_state = self.state.get(p, [])\n   126→                if len(p_state) != 0 and not torch.is_tensor(p_state[\"step\"]):\n   127→                    step_val = float(p_state[\"step\"])\n   128→                    p_state[\"step\"] = (\n   129→                        torch.tensor(\n   130→                            step_val,\n   131→                            dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(is_fused=fused),\n   132→                            device=p.device,\n   133→                        )\n   134→                        if group[\"capturable\"] or group[\"fused\"]\n   135→                        else torch.tensor(step_val, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype())\n   136→                    )\n   137→\n   138→    def _init_group(\n   139→        self,\n   140→        group,\n   141→        params_with_grad,\n   142→        grads,\n   143→        exp_avgs,\n   144→        exp_avg_sqs,\n   145→        max_exp_avg_sqs,\n   146→        state_steps,\n   147→    ):\n   148→        has_complex = False\n   149→        for p in group[\"params\"]:\n   150→            if p.grad is not None:\n   151→                has_complex |= torch.is_complex(p)\n   152→                params_with_grad.append(p)\n   153→                if p.grad.is_sparse:\n   154→                    raise RuntimeError(\n   155→                        \"Adam does not support sparse gradients, please consider SparseAdam instead\"\n   156→                    )\n   157→                grads.append(p.grad)\n   158→\n   159→                state = self.state[p]\n   160→                # Lazy state initialization\n   161→                if len(state) == 0:\n   162→                    if group[\"fused\"]:\n   163→                        _device_dtype_check_for_fused(p)\n   164→                    # note(crcrpar): [special device hosting for step]\n   165→                    # Deliberately host `step` on CPU if both capturable and fused are off.\n   166→                    # This is because kernel launches are costly on CUDA and XLA.\n   167→                    state[\"step\"] = (\n   168→                        torch.zeros(\n   169→                            (),\n   170→                            dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(is_fused=group[\"fused\"]),\n   171→                            device=p.device,\n   172→                        )\n   173→                        if group[\"capturable\"] or group[\"fused\"]\n   174→                        else torch.tensor(0.0, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype())\n   175→                    )\n   176→                    # Exponential moving average of gradient values\n   177→                    state[\"exp_avg\"] = torch.zeros_like(\n   178→                        p, memory_format=torch.preserve_format\n   179→                    )\n   180→                    # Exponential moving average of squared gradient values\n   181→                    state[\"exp_avg_sq\"] = torch.zeros_like(\n   182→                        p, memory_format=torch.preserve_format\n   183→                    )\n   184→                    if group[\"amsgrad\"]:\n   185→                        # Maintains max of all exp. moving avg. of sq. grad. values\n   186→                        state[\"max_exp_avg_sq\"] = torch.zeros_like(\n   187→                            p, memory_format=torch.preserve_format\n   188→                        )\n   189→\n   190→                exp_avgs.append(state[\"exp_avg\"])\n   191→                exp_avg_sqs.append(state[\"exp_avg_sq\"])\n   192→\n   193→                if group[\"amsgrad\"]:\n   194→                    max_exp_avg_sqs.append(state[\"max_exp_avg_sq\"])\n   195→                if group[\"differentiable\"] and state[\"step\"].requires_grad:\n   196→                    raise RuntimeError(\n   197→                        \"`requires_grad` is not supported for `step` in differentiable mode\"\n   198→                    )\n   199→\n   200→                # Foreach without capturable does not support a tensor lr\n   201→                if (\n   202→                    group[\"foreach\"]\n   203→                    and torch.is_tensor(group[\"lr\"])\n   204→                    and not group[\"capturable\"]\n   205→                ):\n   206→                    raise RuntimeError(\n   207→                        \"lr as a Tensor is not supported for capturable=False and foreach=True\"\n   208→                    )\n   209→\n   210→                state_steps.append(state[\"step\"])\n   211→        return has_complex\n   212→\n   213→    @_use_grad_for_differentiable\n   214→    def step(self, closure=None):\n   215→        \"\"\"Perform a single optimization step.\n   216→\n   217→        Args:\n   218→            closure (Callable, optional): A closure that reevaluates the model\n   219→                and returns the loss.\n   220→        \"\"\"\n   221→        self._cuda_graph_capture_health_check()\n   222→\n   223→        loss = None\n   224→        if closure is not None:\n   225→            with torch.enable_grad():\n   226→                loss = closure()\n   227→\n   228→        for group in self.param_groups:\n   229→            params_with_grad: list[Tensor] = []\n   230→            grads: list[Tensor] = []\n   231→            exp_avgs: list[Tensor] = []\n   232→            exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor] = []\n   233→            max_exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor] = []\n   234→            state_steps: list[Tensor] = []\n   235→            beta1, beta2 = group[\"betas\"]\n   236→\n   237→            has_complex = self._init_group(\n   238→                group,\n   239→                params_with_grad,\n   240→                grads,\n   241→                exp_avgs,\n   242→                exp_avg_sqs,\n   243→                max_exp_avg_sqs,\n   244→                state_steps,\n   245→            )\n   246→\n   247→            adam(\n   248→                params_with_grad,\n   249→                grads,\n   250→                exp_avgs,\n   251→                exp_avg_sqs,\n   252→                max_exp_avg_sqs,\n   253→                state_steps,\n   254→                amsgrad=group[\"amsgrad\"],\n   255→                has_complex=has_complex,\n   256→                beta1=beta1,\n   257→                beta2=beta2,\n   258→                lr=group[\"lr\"],\n   259→                weight_decay=group[\"weight_decay\"],\n   260→                eps=group[\"eps\"],\n   261→                maximize=group[\"maximize\"],\n   262→                foreach=group[\"foreach\"],\n   263→                capturable=group[\"capturable\"],\n   264→                differentiable=group[\"differentiable\"],\n   265→                fused=group[\"fused\"],\n   266→                grad_scale=getattr(self, \"grad_scale\", None),\n   267→                found_inf=getattr(self, \"found_inf\", None),\n   268→                decoupled_weight_decay=group[\"decoupled_weight_decay\"],\n   269→            )\n   270→\n   271→        return loss\n   272→\n   273→\n   274→Adam.__doc__ = (\n   275→    r\"\"\"Implements Adam algorithm.\n   276→\n   277→    .. math::\n   278→       \\begin{aligned}\n   279→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n   280→            &\\textbf{input}      : \\gamma \\text{ (lr)}, \\beta_1, \\beta_2\n   281→                \\text{ (betas)},\\theta_0 \\text{ (params)},f(\\theta) \\text{ (objective)}          \\\\\n   282→            &\\hspace{13mm}      \\lambda \\text{ (weight decay)},  \\: \\textit{amsgrad},\n   283→                \\:\\textit{maximize},  \\: \\epsilon \\text{ (epsilon)}                              \\\\\n   284→            &\\textbf{initialize} :  m_0 \\leftarrow 0 \\text{ ( first moment)},\n   285→                v_0\\leftarrow 0 \\text{ (second moment)},\\: v_0^{max}\\leftarrow 0          \\\\[-1.ex]\n   286→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n   287→            &\\textbf{for} \\: t=1 \\: \\textbf{to} \\: \\ldots \\: \\textbf{do}                         \\\\\n   288→\n   289→            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\textit{maximize}:                                       \\\\\n   290→            &\\hspace{10mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   -\\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})         \\\\\n   291→            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{else}                                                           \\\\\n   292→            &\\hspace{10mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   \\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})          \\\\\n   293→            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\lambda \\neq 0                                           \\\\\n   294→            &\\hspace{10mm} g_t \\leftarrow g_t + \\lambda  \\theta_{t-1}                            \\\\\n   295→            &\\hspace{5mm}m_t           \\leftarrow   \\beta_1 m_{t-1} + (1 - \\beta_1) g_t          \\\\\n   296→            &\\hspace{5mm}v_t           \\leftarrow   \\beta_2 v_{t-1} + (1-\\beta_2) g^2_t          \\\\\n   297→            &\\hspace{5mm}\\widehat{m_t} \\leftarrow   m_t/\\big(1-\\beta_1^t \\big)                   \\\\\n   298→            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{if} \\: amsgrad                                                  \\\\\n   299→            &\\hspace{10mm} v_t^{max} \\leftarrow \\mathrm{max}(v_{t-1}^{max},v_t)                  \\\\\n   300→            &\\hspace{10mm}\\widehat{v_t} \\leftarrow v_t^{max}/\\big(1-\\beta_2^t \\big)              \\\\\n   301→            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{else}                                                           \\\\\n   302→            &\\hspace{10mm}\\widehat{v_t} \\leftarrow   v_t/\\big(1-\\beta_2^t \\big)                  \\\\\n   303→            &\\hspace{5mm}\\theta_t \\leftarrow \\theta_{t-1} - \\gamma \\widehat{m_t}/\n   304→                \\big(\\sqrt{\\widehat{v_t}} + \\epsilon \\big)                                       \\\\\n   305→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n   306→            &\\bf{return} \\:  \\theta_t                                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n   307→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n   308→       \\end{aligned}\n   309→\n   310→    For further details regarding the algorithm we refer to `Adam: A Method for Stochastic Optimization`_.\n   311→    \"\"\"\n   312→    + rf\"\"\"\n   313→    Args:\n   314→        {_params_doc}\n   315→        lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 1e-3). A tensor LR\n   316→            is not yet supported for all our implementations. Please use a float\n   317→            LR if you are not also specifying fused=True or capturable=True.\n   318→        betas (Tuple[float, float], optional): coefficients used for computing\n   319→            running averages of gradient and its square (default: (0.9, 0.999))\n   320→        eps (float, optional): term added to the denominator to improve\n   321→            numerical stability (default: 1e-8)\n   322→        weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay (L2 penalty) (default: 0)\n   323→        decoupled_weight_decay (bool, optional): if True, this optimizer is\n   324→            equivalent to AdamW and the algorithm will not accumulate weight\n   325→            decay in the momentum nor variance. (default: False)\n   326→        amsgrad (bool, optional): whether to use the AMSGrad variant of this\n   327→            algorithm from the paper `On the Convergence of Adam and Beyond`_\n   328→            (default: False)\n   329→        {_foreach_doc}\n   330→        {_maximize_doc}\n   331→        {_capturable_doc}\n   332→        {_differentiable_doc}\n   333→        {_fused_doc}\n   334→    .. Note::\n   335→        A prototype implementation of Adam and AdamW for MPS supports `torch.float32` and `torch.float16`.\n   336→    .. _Adam\\: A Method for Stochastic Optimization:\n   337→        https://arxiv.org/abs/1412.6980\n   338→    .. _On the Convergence of Adam and Beyond:\n   339→        https://openreview.net/forum?id=ryQu7f-RZ\n   340→\n   341→    \"\"\"\n   342→)\n   343→\n   344→\n   345→def _single_tensor_adam(\n   346→    params: list[Tensor],\n   347→    grads: list[Tensor],\n   348→    exp_avgs: list[Tensor],\n   349→    exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],\n   350→    max_exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],\n   351→    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n   352→    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\n   353→    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\n   354→    *,\n   355→    amsgrad: bool,\n   356→    has_complex: bool,\n   357→    beta1: Union[float, Tensor],\n   358→    beta2: Union[float, Tensor],\n   359→    lr: Union[float, Tensor],\n   360→    weight_decay: float,\n   361→    eps: float,\n   362→    maximize: bool,\n   363→    capturable: bool,\n   364→    differentiable: bool,\n   365→    decoupled_weight_decay: bool,\n   366→):\n   367→    assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None\n   368→\n   369→    if torch.jit.is_scripting():\n   370→        # this assert is due to JIT being dumb and not realizing that the ops below\n   371→        # have overloads to handle both float and Tensor lrs, so we just assert it's\n   372→        # a float since most people using JIT are using floats\n   373→        assert isinstance(lr, float)\n   374→        assert isinstance(beta1, float)\n   375→        assert isinstance(beta2, float)\n   376→    else:\n   377→        lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n   378→        # TODO: Support nonzero-dim Tensor betas, see #147921\n   379→\n   380→    # We only shuffle around the beta when it is a Tensor, otherwise, we prefer\n   381→    # treating it as a scalar.\n   382→    # Note: ensure type declaration is under conditional check for isinstance\n   383→    # or else torchscript will get cranky about the DeviceDict type.\n   384→    if isinstance(beta1, Tensor):\n   385→        beta1_dict: Optional[DeviceDtypeDict] = {(beta1.device, beta1.dtype): beta1}\n   386→    else:\n   387→        beta1_dict = None\n   388→\n   389→    for i, param in enumerate(params):\n   390→        grad = grads[i] if not maximize else -grads[i]\n   391→        exp_avg = exp_avgs[i]\n   392→        exp_avg_sq = exp_avg_sqs[i]\n   393→        step_t = state_steps[i]\n   394→\n   395→        # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n   396→        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n   397→            capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices()\n   398→            assert (\n   399→                param.device.type == step_t.device.type\n   400→                and param.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n   401→            ), (\n   402→                f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n   403→            )\n   404→\n   405→        # update step\n   406→        step_t += 1\n   407→\n   408→        if weight_decay != 0:\n   409→            if decoupled_weight_decay:\n   410→                # Perform stepweight decay\n   411→                param.mul_(1 - lr * weight_decay)\n   412→            else:\n   413→                # Nested if is necessary to bypass jitscript rules\n   414→                if differentiable and isinstance(weight_decay, Tensor):\n   415→                    if weight_decay.requires_grad:\n   416→                        grad = grad.addcmul_(param.clone(), weight_decay)\n   417→                    else:\n   418→                        grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\n   419→                else:\n   420→                    grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\n   421→\n   422→        if torch.is_complex(param):\n   423→            grad = torch.view_as_real(grad)\n   424→            exp_avg = torch.view_as_real(exp_avg)\n   425→            exp_avg_sq = torch.view_as_real(exp_avg_sq)\n   426→            if amsgrad:\n   427→                max_exp_avg_sqs[i] = torch.view_as_real(max_exp_avg_sqs[i])\n   428→            param = torch.view_as_real(param)\n   429→\n   430→        device = param.device\n   431→\n   432→        if beta1_dict is not None:\n   433→            dtype = param.dtype  # type: ignore[union-attr]\n   434→\n   435→            # cast to workaround https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/140601\n   436→            key = (device, dtype)\n   437→            if key not in beta1_dict:\n   438→                beta1_dict[key] = beta1.to(  # type: ignore[union-attr]\n   439→                    device=device, dtype=dtype, non_blocking=True\n   440→                )\n   441→\n   442→            device_beta1: Union[float, Tensor] = beta1_dict[key]\n   443→        else:\n   444→            device_beta1 = beta1\n   445→\n   446→        # Decay the first and second moment running average coefficient\n   447→        exp_avg.lerp_(grad, 1 - device_beta1)\n   448→\n   449→        # Nested if is necessary to bypass jitscript rules\n   450→        if differentiable and isinstance(beta2, Tensor):\n   451→            if beta2.requires_grad:\n   452→                # Using lerp to only use 2 operations bc addcmul's value cannot be a tensor\n   453→                # Showing equivalence of differentiable path and nondifferentiable path\n   454→                # expavg * b2 + grad^2 * (1-b2)\n   455→                #           add expavg * (1-b2) - expavg * (1-b2) = 0\n   456→                # expavg * b2 + expavg * (1-b2) - expavg * (1-b2) + grad^2 * (1-b2)\n   457→                # expavg - expavg * (1-b2) + grad^2 * (1-b2)\n   458→                # expavg + (grad^2 - expavg) * (1-b2)\n   459→                # expavg.lerp(grad^2, 1-beta2)\n   460→                exp_avg_sq.lerp_(torch.square(grad), weight=1 - beta2)\n   461→            else:\n   462→                exp_avg_sq.mul_(beta2).addcmul_(\n   463→                    grad, grad, value=cast(float, 1 - beta2)\n   464→                )\n   465→        else:\n   466→            exp_avg_sq.mul_(beta2).addcmul_(grad, grad, value=1 - beta2)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n   467→\n   468→        if capturable or differentiable:\n   469→            step = step_t\n   470→\n   471→            # Nested if is necessary to bypass jitscript rules\n   472→            if differentiable and isinstance(beta1, Tensor):\n   473→                if beta1.requires_grad:\n   474→                    bias_correction1 = 1 - beta1 ** step.clone()\n   475→                else:\n   476→                    bias_correction1 = 1 - beta1**step\n   477→            else:\n   478→                bias_correction1 = 1 - beta1**step\n   479→\n   480→            # Nested if is necessary to bypass jitscript rules\n   481→            if differentiable and isinstance(beta2, Tensor):\n   482→                if beta2.requires_grad:\n   483→                    bias_correction2 = 1 - beta2 ** step.clone()\n   484→                else:\n   485→                    bias_correction2 = 1 - beta2**step\n   486→            else:\n   487→                bias_correction2 = 1 - beta2**step\n   488→\n   489→            step_size = lr / bias_correction1\n   490→            step_size_neg = step_size.neg()\n   491→\n   492→            bias_correction2_sqrt = bias_correction2.sqrt()\n   493→\n   494→            if amsgrad:\n   495→                # Maintains the maximum of all 2nd moment running avg. till now\n   496→                if differentiable:\n   497→                    max_exp_avg_sq = max_exp_avg_sqs[i].clone()\n   498→                else:\n   499→                    max_exp_avg_sq = max_exp_avg_sqs[i]\n   500→\n   501→                max_exp_avg_sqs[i].copy_(torch.maximum(max_exp_avg_sq, exp_avg_sq))\n   502→\n   503→                # Uses the max. for normalizing running avg. of gradient\n   504→                # Folds in (admittedly ugly) 1-elem step_size math here to avoid extra param-set-sized read+write\n   505→                # (can't fold it into addcdiv_ below because addcdiv_ requires value is a Number, not a Tensor)\n   506→                denom = (\n   507→                    max_exp_avg_sqs[i].sqrt() / (bias_correction2_sqrt * step_size_neg)\n   508→                ).add_(eps / step_size_neg)\n   509→            else:\n   510→                denom = (\n   511→                    exp_avg_sq.sqrt() / (bias_correction2_sqrt * step_size_neg)\n   512→                ).add_(eps / step_size_neg)\n   513→\n   514→            if differentiable:\n   515→                param.addcdiv_(exp_avg.clone(), denom)\n   516→            else:\n   517→                param.addcdiv_(exp_avg, denom)\n   518→        else:\n   519→            step = _get_value(step_t)\n   520→\n   521→            bias_correction1 = 1 - beta1**step\n   522→            bias_correction2 = 1 - beta2**step\n   523→\n   524→            step_size = lr / bias_correction1\n   525→\n   526→            bias_correction2_sqrt = bias_correction2**0.5\n   527→\n   528→            if amsgrad:\n   529→                # Maintains the maximum of all 2nd moment running avg. till now\n   530→                torch.maximum(max_exp_avg_sqs[i], exp_avg_sq, out=max_exp_avg_sqs[i])\n   531→\n   532→                # Use the max. for normalizing running avg. of gradient\n   533→                denom = (max_exp_avg_sqs[i].sqrt() / bias_correction2_sqrt).add_(eps)\n   534→            else:\n   535→                denom = (exp_avg_sq.sqrt() / bias_correction2_sqrt).add_(eps)\n   536→\n   537→            param.addcdiv_(exp_avg, denom, value=-step_size)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n   538→\n   539→        # Lastly, switch back to complex view\n   540→        if amsgrad and torch.is_complex(params[i]):\n   541→            max_exp_avg_sqs[i] = torch.view_as_complex(max_exp_avg_sqs[i])\n   542→\n   543→\n   544→def _multi_tensor_adam(\n   545→    params: list[Tensor],\n   546→    grads: list[Tensor],\n   547→    exp_avgs: list[Tensor],\n   548→    exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],\n   549→    max_exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],\n   550→    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n   551→    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\n   552→    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\n   553→    *,\n   554→    amsgrad: bool,\n   555→    has_complex: bool,\n   556→    beta1: Union[float, Tensor],\n   557→    beta2: Union[float, Tensor],\n   558→    lr: Union[float, Tensor],\n   559→    weight_decay: float,\n   560→    eps: float,\n   561→    maximize: bool,\n   562→    capturable: bool,\n   563→    differentiable: bool,\n   564→    decoupled_weight_decay: bool,\n   565→):\n   566→    if len(params) == 0:\n   567→        return\n   568→\n   569→    if isinstance(lr, Tensor):\n   570→        if not capturable:\n   571→            raise RuntimeError(\n   572→                \"lr as a Tensor is not supported for capturable=False and foreach=True\"\n   573→            )\n   574→        if lr.numel() != 1:\n   575→            raise ValueError(\"Tensor lr must be 1-element\")\n   576→\n   577→    if isinstance(beta1, Tensor):\n   578→        if not capturable:\n   579→            raise ValueError(\n   580→                \"beta1 as a Tensor is not supported for capturable=False and foreach=True\"\n   581→            )\n   582→        if beta1.numel() != 1:\n   583→            raise ValueError(\"Tensor beta1 must be 1-element\")\n   584→\n   585→    if isinstance(beta2, Tensor):\n   586→        if not capturable:\n   587→            raise ValueError(\n   588→                \"beta2 as a Tensor is not supported for capturable=False and foreach=True\"\n   589→            )\n   590→        if beta2.numel() != 1:\n   591→            raise ValueError(\"Tensor beta2 must be 1-element\")\n   592→\n   593→    # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n   594→    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n   595→        capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices(\n   596→            supports_xla=False\n   597→        )\n   598→        assert all(\n   599→            p.device.type == step.device.type\n   600→            and p.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n   601→            for p, step in zip(params, state_steps)\n   602→        ), (\n   603→            f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n   604→        )\n   605→\n   606→    assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None\n   607→\n   608→    assert not differentiable, \"_foreach ops don't support autograd\"\n   609→\n   610→    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n   611→    # TODO: Support nonzero-dim Tensor betas, see #147921\n   612→\n   613→    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n   614→        [params, grads, exp_avgs, exp_avg_sqs, max_exp_avg_sqs, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n   615→    )\n   616→\n   617→    # We only shuffle around the beta when it is a Tensor and on CUDA, otherwise, we prefer\n   618→    # treating it as a scalar.\n   619→    beta1_dict: Optional[DeviceDict] = (  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n   620→        {beta1.device: beta1}\n   621→        if isinstance(beta1, Tensor) and str(beta1.device) != \"cpu\"\n   622→        else None\n   623→    )\n   624→\n   625→    for (\n   626→        device_params_,\n   627→        device_grads_,\n   628→        device_exp_avgs_,\n   629→        device_exp_avg_sqs_,\n   630→        device_max_exp_avg_sqs_,\n   631→        device_state_steps_,\n   632→    ), _ in grouped_tensors.values():\n   633→        device_params = cast(list[Tensor], device_params_)\n   634→        device_grads = cast(list[Tensor], device_grads_)\n   635→        device_exp_avgs = cast(list[Tensor], device_exp_avgs_)\n   636→        device_exp_avg_sqs = cast(list[Tensor], device_exp_avg_sqs_)\n   637→        device_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], device_state_steps_)\n   638→\n   639→        device = device_params[0].device\n   640→        if beta1_dict is not None and device not in beta1_dict:\n   641→            beta1_dict[device] = beta1.to(device=device, non_blocking=True)  # type: ignore[union-attr, attr-defined]\n   642→\n   643→        device_beta1 = beta1_dict[device] if beta1_dict else beta1\n   644→\n   645→        # Handle complex parameters\n   646→        if has_complex:\n   647→            if amsgrad:\n   648→                device_max_exp_avg_sqs = cast(list[Tensor], device_max_exp_avg_sqs_)\n   649→                _view_as_real(\n   650→                    device_params,\n   651→                    device_grads,\n   652→                    device_exp_avgs,\n   653→                    device_exp_avg_sqs,\n   654→                    device_max_exp_avg_sqs,\n   655→                )\n   656→            else:\n   657→                _view_as_real(\n   658→                    device_params, device_grads, device_exp_avgs, device_exp_avg_sqs\n   659→                )\n   660→\n   661→        if maximize:\n   662→            device_grads = torch._foreach_neg(device_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n   663→\n   664→        # Update steps\n   665→        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n   666→        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n   667→        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n   668→        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and device_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n   669→            torch._foreach_add_(\n   670→                device_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n   671→            )\n   672→        else:\n   673→            torch._foreach_add_(device_state_steps, 1)\n   674→\n   675→        if weight_decay != 0:\n   676→            if decoupled_weight_decay:\n   677→                # Perform stepweight decay\n   678→                torch._foreach_mul_(device_params, 1 - lr * weight_decay)\n   679→            else:\n   680→                # Reuse the intermediate memory (device_grads) already allocated for maximize\n   681→                if maximize:\n   682→                    torch._foreach_add_(device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay)\n   683→                else:\n   684→                    device_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]\n   685→                        device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay\n   686→                    )\n   687→\n   688→        # Decay the first and second moment running average coefficient\n   689→        # Use device beta1 if beta1 is a tensor to ensure all\n   690→        # tensors are on the same device\n   691→        torch._foreach_lerp_(\n   692→            device_exp_avgs, device_grads, cast(float, 1 - device_beta1)\n   693→        )\n   694→\n   695→        torch._foreach_mul_(device_exp_avg_sqs, beta2)\n   696→\n   697→        # Due to the strictness of the _foreach_addcmul API, we can't have a single\n   698→        # tensor scalar as the scalar arg (only python number is supported there)\n   699→        # as a result, separate out the value mul\n   700→        # Filed https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/139795\n   701→        if isinstance(beta2, torch.Tensor):\n   702→            scaled_device_grads = torch._foreach_mul(device_grads, 1 - beta2)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n   703→            value = 1.0\n   704→        else:\n   705→            scaled_device_grads = device_grads  # type: ignore[assignment]\n   706→            value = 1 - beta2\n   707→\n   708→        torch._foreach_addcmul_(\n   709→            device_exp_avg_sqs, scaled_device_grads, device_grads, value\n   710→        )\n   711→\n   712→        # Delete the local intermediate(s) since they won't be used anymore to save on peak memory\n   713→        del device_grads\n   714→        del scaled_device_grads\n   715→\n   716→        bias_correction1: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n   717→        bias_correction2: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n   718→        bias_correction2_sqrt: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n   719→\n   720→        if capturable:\n   721→            bias_correction1 = torch._foreach_pow(beta1, device_state_steps)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n   722→            bias_correction2 = torch._foreach_pow(beta2, device_state_steps)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n   723→            # foreach_sub doesn't allow a scalar as the first arg\n   724→            torch._foreach_sub_(bias_correction1, 1)\n   725→            torch._foreach_sub_(bias_correction2, 1)\n   726→            # we do not negate bias_correction1 as it'll need to be negated later anyway\n   727→            torch._foreach_neg_(bias_correction2)\n   728→\n   729→            # foreach_div doesn't allow a scalar as the first arg\n   730→            torch._foreach_div_(bias_correction1, lr)\n   731→            torch._foreach_reciprocal_(bias_correction1)\n   732→\n   733→            torch._foreach_sqrt_(bias_correction2)\n   734→\n   735→            # Re-assign for clarity as we maintain minimal intermediates: we'll have\n   736→            # step_size = - lr / (1 - beta1 ^ t) where t = num_steps\n   737→            # bias_correction2_sqrt = sqrt(1 - beta2 ^ t)\n   738→            step_size = bias_correction1\n   739→            bias_correction2_sqrt = bias_correction2\n   740→\n   741→            if amsgrad:\n   742→                device_max_exp_avg_sqs = cast(list[Tensor], device_max_exp_avg_sqs_)\n   743→                # Maintains the maximum of all 2nd moment running avg. till now\n   744→                torch._foreach_maximum_(device_max_exp_avg_sqs, device_exp_avg_sqs)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n   745→\n   746→                # Set intermediate to the max. for normalizing running avg. of gradient when amsgrad\n   747→                exp_avg_sq_sqrt = torch._foreach_sqrt(device_max_exp_avg_sqs)\n   748→            else:\n   749→                exp_avg_sq_sqrt = torch._foreach_sqrt(device_exp_avg_sqs)\n   750→\n   751→            torch._foreach_div_(exp_avg_sq_sqrt, bias_correction2_sqrt)\n   752→            torch._foreach_add_(exp_avg_sq_sqrt, eps)\n   753→            torch._foreach_div_(exp_avg_sq_sqrt, step_size)\n   754→\n   755→            # at this point, exp_avg_sq_sqrt = - (1 - beta^t) * [sqrt(exp_avg_sq / (1 - beta2^t)) + eps] / lr\n   756→            torch._foreach_addcdiv_(device_params, device_exp_avgs, exp_avg_sq_sqrt)\n   757→        else:\n   758→            bias_correction1 = [\n   759→                1 - beta1 ** _get_value(step) for step in device_state_steps\n   760→            ]\n   761→            bias_correction2 = [\n   762→                1 - beta2 ** _get_value(step) for step in device_state_steps\n   763→            ]\n   764→\n   765→            step_size = _stack_if_compiling([(lr / bc) * -1 for bc in bias_correction1])\n   766→\n   767→            bias_correction2_sqrt = [bc**0.5 for bc in bias_correction2]  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n   768→\n   769→            if amsgrad:\n   770→                device_max_exp_avg_sqs = cast(list[Tensor], device_max_exp_avg_sqs_)\n   771→                # Maintains the maximum of all 2nd moment running avg. till now\n   772→                torch._foreach_maximum_(device_max_exp_avg_sqs, device_exp_avg_sqs)\n   773→\n   774→                # memory_format=torch.preserve_format\n   148→                    )\n   149→\n   150→                exp_avgs.append(state[\"exp_avg\"])\n   151→                exp_avg_sqs.append(state[\"exp_avg_sq\"])\n   152→                mu_products.append(state[\"mu_product\"])\n   153→                state_steps.append(state[\"step\"])\n   154→        return has_complex\n   155→\n   156→    @_use_grad_for_differentiable\n   157→    def step(self, closure=None):\n   158→        \"\"\"Perform a single optimization step.\n   159→\n   160→        Args:\n   161→            closure (Callable, optional): A closure that reevaluates the model\n   162→                and returns the loss.\n   163→        \"\"\"\n   164→        self._cuda_graph_capture_health_check()\n   165→\n   166→        loss = None\n   167→        if closure is not None:\n   168→            with torch.enable_grad():\n   169→                loss = closure()\n   170→\n   171→        for group in self.param_groups:\n   172→            params_with_grad: list[Tensor] = []\n   173→            grads: list[Tensor] = []\n   174→            exp_avgs: list[Tensor] = []\n   175→            exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor] = []\n   176→            mu_products: list[Tensor] = []\n   177→            state_steps: list[Tensor] = []\n   178→            beta1, beta2 = cast(tuple[float, float], group[\"betas\"])\n   179→\n   180→            has_complex = self._init_group(\n   181→                group,\n   182→                params_with_grad,\n   183→                grads,\n   184→                exp_avgs,\n   185→                exp_avg_sqs,\n   186→                mu_products,\n   187→                state_steps,\n   188→            )\n   189→\n   190→            nadam(\n   191→                params_with_grad,\n   192→                grads,\n   193→                exp_avgs,\n   194→                exp_avg_sqs,\n   195→                mu_products,\n   196→                state_steps,\n   197→                beta1=beta1,\n   198→                beta2=beta2,\n   199→                lr=group[\"lr\"],\n   200→                weight_decay=group[\"weight_decay\"],\n   201→                momentum_decay=group[\"momentum_decay\"],\n   202→                eps=group[\"eps\"],\n   203→                maximize=group[\"maximize\"],\n   204→                decoupled_weight_decay=group[\"decoupled_weight_decay\"],\n   205→                foreach=group[\"foreach\"],\n   206→                capturable=group[\"capturable\"],\n   207→                differentiable=group[\"differentiable\"],\n   208→                has_complex=has_complex,\n   209→            )\n   210→\n   211→        return loss\n   212→\n   213→\n   214→NAdam.__doc__ = (\n   215→    r\"\"\"Implements NAdam algorithm.\n   216→\n   217→    .. math::\n   218→       \\begin{aligned}\n   219→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n   220→            &\\textbf{input}      : \\gamma_t \\text{ (lr)}, \\: \\beta_1,\\beta_2 \\text{ (betas)},\n   221→                \\: \\theta_0 \\text{ (params)}, \\: f(\\theta) \\text{ (objective)}                   \\\\\n   222→            &\\hspace{13mm} \\: \\lambda \\text{ (weight decay)}, \\:\\psi \\text{ (momentum decay)}    \\\\\n   223→            &\\hspace{13mm} \\: \\textit{decoupled\\_weight\\_decay}, \\:\\textit{maximize}             \\\\\n   224→            &\\textbf{initialize} :  m_0 \\leftarrow 0 \\text{ ( first moment)},\n   225→                v_0 \\leftarrow 0 \\text{ ( second moment)}                                 \\\\[-1.ex]\n   226→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n   227→            &\\textbf{for} \\: t=1 \\: \\textbf{to} \\: \\ldots \\: \\textbf{do}                         \\\\\n   228→            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\textit{maximize}:                                       \\\\\n   229→            &\\hspace{10mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   -\\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})         \\\\\n   230→            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{else}                                                           \\\\\n   231→            &\\hspace{10mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   \\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})          \\\\\n   232→            &\\hspace{5mm} \\theta_t \\leftarrow \\theta_{t-1}                                       \\\\\n   233→            &\\hspace{5mm} \\textbf{if} \\: \\lambda \\neq 0                                          \\\\\n   234→            &\\hspace{10mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\textit{decoupled\\_weight\\_decay}                       \\\\\n   235→            &\\hspace{15mm} \\theta_t \\leftarrow \\theta_{t-1} - \\gamma \\lambda \\theta_{t-1}                    \\\\\n   236→            &\\hspace{10mm}\\textbf{else}                                                          \\\\\n   237→            &\\hspace{15mm} g_t \\leftarrow g_t + \\lambda \\theta_{t-1}                             \\\\\n   238→            &\\hspace{5mm} \\mu_t \\leftarrow \\beta_1 \\big(1 - \\frac{1}{2}  0.96^{t \\psi} \\big)     \\\\\n   239→            &\\hspace{5mm} \\mu_{t+1} \\leftarrow \\beta_1 \\big(1 - \\frac{1}{2} 0.96^{(t+1)\\psi}\\big)\\\\\n   240→            &\\hspace{5mm}m_t           \\leftarrow   \\beta_1 m_{t-1} + (1 - \\beta_1) g_t          \\\\\n   241→            &\\hspace{5mm}v_t           \\leftarrow   \\beta_2 v_{t-1} + (1-\\beta_2) g^2_t          \\\\\n   242→            &\\hspace{5mm}\\widehat{m_t} \\leftarrow \\mu_{t+1} m_t/(1-\\prod_{i=1}^{t+1}\\mu_i)\\\\[-1.ex]\n   243→            & \\hspace{11mm} + (1-\\mu_t) g_t /(1-\\prod_{i=1}^{t} \\mu_{i})                         \\\\\n   244→            &\\hspace{5mm}\\widehat{v_t} \\leftarrow   v_t/\\big(1-\\beta_2^t \\big)                   \\\\\n   245→            &\\hspace{5mm}\\theta_t \\leftarrow \\theta_t - \\gamma \\widehat{m_t}/\n   246→                \\big(\\sqrt{\\widehat{v_t}} + \\epsilon \\big)                                       \\\\\n   247→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n   248→            &\\bf{return} \\:  \\theta_t                                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n   249→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n   250→       \\end{aligned}\n   251→\n   252→    For further details regarding the algorithm we refer to `Incorporating Nesterov Momentum into Adam`_.\n   253→    \"\"\"\n   254→    + rf\"\"\"\n   255→    Args:\n   256→        {_params_doc}\n   257→        lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 2e-3)\n   258→        betas (Tuple[float, float], optional): coefficients used for computing\n   259→            running averages of gradient and its square (default: (0.9, 0.999))\n   260→        eps (float, optional): term added to the denominator to improve\n   261→            numerical stability (default: 1e-8)\n   262→        weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay (L2 penalty) (default: 0)\n   263→        momentum_decay (float, optional): momentum momentum_decay (default: 4e-3)\n   264→        decoupled_weight_decay (bool, optional): whether to decouple the weight\n   265→            decay as in AdamW to obtain NAdamW. If True, the algorithm does not\n   266→            accumulate weight decay in the momentum nor variance. (default: False)\n   267→        {_foreach_doc}\n   268→        {_maximize_doc}\n   269→        {_capturable_doc}\n   270→        {_differentiable_doc}\n   271→\n   272→    .. _Incorporating Nesterov Momentum into Adam:\n   273→        https://openreview.net/forum?id=OM0jvwB8jIp57ZJjtNEZ\n   274→    .. _Decoupled Weight Decay Regularization:\n   275→        https://arxiv.org/abs/1711.05101\n   276→\n   277→    \"\"\"\n   278→)\n   279→\n   280→\n   281→def _single_tensor_nadam(\n   282→    params: list[Tensor],\n   283→    grads: list[Tensor],\n   284→    exp_avgs: list[Tensor],\n   285→    exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],\n   286→    mu_products: list[Tensor],\n   287→    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n   288→    *,\n   289→    beta1: float,\n   290→    beta2: float,\n   291→    lr: float,\n   292→    weight_decay: float,\n   293→    momentum_decay: float,\n   294→    eps: float,\n   295→    decoupled_weight_decay: bool,\n   296→    maximize: bool,\n   297→    capturable: bool,\n   298→    differentiable: bool,\n   299→    has_complex: bool,\n   300→):\n   301→    if not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n   302→        lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n   303→\n   304→    for i, param in enumerate(params):\n   305→        grad = grads[i] if not maximize else -grads[i]\n   306→        exp_avg = exp_avgs[i]\n   307→        exp_avg_sq = exp_avg_sqs[i]\n   308→        mu_product = mu_products[i]\n   309→        step_t = state_steps[i]\n   310→\n   311→        if torch.is_complex(param):\n   312→            param = torch.view_as_real(param)\n   313→            grad = torch.view_as_real(grad)\n   314→            exp_avg = torch.view_as_real(exp_avg)\n   315→            exp_avg_sq = torch.view_as_real(exp_avg_sq)\n   316→\n   317→        # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n   318→        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n   319→            capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices()\n   320→            assert (\n   321→                param.device.type == mu_product.device.type == step_t.device.type\n   322→                and param.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n   323→            ), (\n   324→                f\"If capturable=True, params, mu_products and state_steps must be \"\n   325→                f\"on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n   326→            )\n   327→\n   328→        # update step\n   329→        step_t += 1\n   330→\n   331→        if capturable:\n   332→            step = step_t\n   333→        else:\n   334→            step = _get_value(step_t)\n   335→\n   336→        bias_correction2 = 1 - beta2**step\n   337→\n   338→        if weight_decay != 0:\n   339→            if decoupled_weight_decay:\n   340→                # Perform stepweight decay\n   341→                param.mul_(1 - lr * weight_decay)\n   342→            else:\n   343→                grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\n   344→\n   345→        # calculate the momentum cache \\mu^{t} and \\mu^{t+1}\n   346→        mu = beta1 * (1.0 - 0.5 * (0.96 ** (step * momentum_decay)))\n   347→        mu_next = beta1 * (1.0 - 0.5 * (0.96 ** ((step + 1) * momentum_decay)))\n   348→\n   349→        # update mu_product\n   350→        mu_product *= mu\n   351→\n   352→        # decay the first and second moment running average coefficient\n   353→        exp_avg.lerp_(grad, 1 - beta1)\n   354→        exp_avg_sq.mul_(beta2).addcmul_(grad, grad, value=1 - beta2)\n   355→        denom = exp_avg_sq.div(bias_correction2).sqrt()\n   356→\n   357→        if differentiable or capturable:\n   358→            denom = denom.add(eps)\n   359→            # Make autograd track the operations\n   360→            # by updating the grad and exp_avg directly and not using the\n   361→            # scalar \"value\" argument of addcdiv.\n   362→            mu_product_next = mu_product * mu_next\n   363→            grad = grad * (-lr * (1.0 - mu) / (1.0 - mu_product))\n   364→            exp_avg = exp_avg * (-lr * mu_next / (1.0 - mu_product_next))\n   365→            param.addcdiv_(grad, denom)\n   366→            param.addcdiv_(exp_avg, denom)\n   367→        else:\n   368→            mu_product_next = _get_value(mu_product) * mu_next\n   369→            denom.add_(eps)\n   370→            param.addcdiv_(\n   371→                grad, denom, value=(-lr * (1.0 - mu) / (1.0 - _get_value(mu_product)))\n   372→            )\n   373→            param.addcdiv_(\n   374→                exp_avg,\n   375→                denom,\n   376→                value=cast(float, (-lr * mu_next) / (1.0 - mu_product_next)),\n   377→            )\n   378→\n   379→\n   380→def _multi_tensor_nadam(\n   381→    params: list[Tensor],\n   382→    grads: list[Tensor],\n   383→    exp_avgs: list[Tensor],\n   384→    exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],\n   385→    mu_products: list[Tensor],\n   386→    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n   387→    *,\n   388→    beta1: float,\n   389→    beta2: float,\n   390→    lr: float,\n   391→    weight_decay: float,\n   392→    momentum_decay: float,\n   393→    eps: float,\n   394→    decoupled_weight_decay: bool,\n   395→    maximize: bool,\n   396→    capturable: bool,\n   397→    differentiable: bool,\n   398→    has_complex: bool,\n   399→):\n   400→    if len(params) == 0:\n   401→        return\n   402→\n   403→    assert not differentiable, \"_foreach ops don't support autograd\"\n   404→\n   405→    # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n   406→    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n   407→        capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices(\n   408→            supports_xla=False\n   409→        )\n   410→        assert all(\n   411→            p.device.type == mp.device.type == step.device.type\n   412→            and p.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n   413→            for p, mp, step in zip(params, mu_products, state_steps)\n   414→        ), (\n   415→            \"If capturable=True, \"\n   416→            \"params, mu_products, and state_steps must be on supported devices: \"\n   417→            f\"{capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n   418→        )\n   419→\n   420→    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n   421→\n   422→    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n   423→        [params, grads, exp_avgs, exp_avg_sqs, mu_products, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n   424→    )\n   425→    for (\n   426→        grouped_params_,\n   427→        grouped_grads_,\n   428→        grouped_exp_avgs_,\n   429→        grouped_exp_avg_sqs_,\n   430→        grouped_mu_products_,\n   431→        grouped_state_steps_,\n   432→    ), _ in grouped_tensors.values():\n   433→        grouped_params = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_params_)\n   434→        grouped_grads = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_grads_)\n   435→        grouped_exp_avgs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_exp_avgs_)\n   436→        grouped_exp_avg_sqs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_exp_avg_sqs_)\n   437→        grouped_mu_products = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_mu_products_)\n   438→        grouped_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_state_steps_)\n   439→\n   440→        # handle complex\n   441→        if has_complex:\n   442→            _view_as_real(\n   443→                grouped_params, grouped_grads, grouped_exp_avgs, grouped_exp_avg_sqs\n   444→            )\n   445→\n   446→        if maximize:\n   447→            grouped_grads = torch._foreach_neg(grouped_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n   448→\n   449→        # Update steps\n   450→        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n   451→        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n   452→        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n   453→        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and grouped_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n   454→            torch._foreach_add_(\n   455→                grouped_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n   456→            )\n   457→        else:\n   458→            torch._foreach_add_(grouped_state_steps, 1)\n   459→\n   460→        if weight_decay != 0:\n   461→            if decoupled_weight_decay:\n   462→                # Perform stepweight decay\n   463→                torch._foreach_mul_(grouped_params, 1 - lr * weight_decay)\n   464→            else:\n   465→                # Reuse the intermediate memory (grouped_grads) already allocated for maximize\n   466→                if maximize:\n   467→                    torch._foreach_add_(\n   468→                        grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay\n   469→                    )\n   470→                else:\n   471→                    grouped_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]\n   472→                        grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay\n   473→                    )\n   474→\n   475→        # Decay the first and second moment running average coefficient\n   476→        torch._foreach_lerp_(grouped_exp_avgs, grouped_grads, 1 - beta1)\n   477→\n   478→        torch._foreach_mul_(grouped_exp_avg_sqs, beta2)\n   479→        torch._foreach_addcmul_(\n   480→            grouped_exp_avg_sqs, grouped_grads, grouped_grads, 1 - beta2\n   481→        )\n   482→\n   483→        exp_avg_sq_sqrt = torch._foreach_sqrt(grouped_exp_avg_sqs)\n   484→\n   485→        bias_correction_sqrt: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n   486→        mus: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n   487→        mu_nexts: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n   488→        if capturable:\n   489→            # mus will be beta1 * (1 - 0.5 * 0.96 ** (step * momentum_decay))\n   490→            exponent = torch._foreach_mul(grouped_state_steps, momentum_decay)\n   491→            mus = torch._foreach_pow(0.96, exponent)\n   492→            torch._foreach_mul_(mus, -0.5)\n   493→            torch._foreach_add_(mus, 1.0)\n   494→            torch._foreach_mul_(mus, beta1)\n   495→\n   496→            # mu_nexts will be beta1 * (1 - 0.5 * 0.96 ** ((step + 1) * momentum_decay))\n   497→            torch._foreach_add_(exponent, momentum_decay)\n   498→            mu_nexts = torch._foreach_pow(0.96, exponent)\n   499→            torch._foreach_mul_(mu_nexts, -0.5)\n   500→            torch._foreach_add_(mu_nexts, 1.0)\n   501→            torch._foreach_mul_(mu_nexts, beta1)\n   502→\n   503→            # save peak memory as we don't need exponent anymore\n   504→            del exponent\n   505→\n   506→            bias_correction_sqrt = torch._foreach_pow(beta2, grouped_state_steps)\n   507→            # foreach_sub doesn't allow a scalar as the first arg\n   508→            torch._foreach_sub_(bias_correction_sqrt, 1.0)\n   509→            torch._foreach_neg_(bias_correction_sqrt)\n   510→            torch._foreach_sqrt_(bias_correction_sqrt)\n   511→        else:\n   512→            bias_correction_sqrt = [\n   513→                (1 - beta2 ** _get_value(step)) ** 0.5 for step in grouped_state_steps\n   514→            ]\n   515→            mus = [\n   516→                beta1 * (1.0 - 0.5 * (0.96 ** (_get_value(step) * momentum_decay)))\n   517→                for step in grouped_state_steps\n   518→            ]\n   519→            mu_nexts = [\n   520→                beta1\n   521→                * (1.0 - 0.5 * (0.96 ** ((_get_value(step) + 1) * momentum_decay)))\n   522→                for step in grouped_state_steps\n   523→            ]\n   524→\n   525→        # update mu_products\n   526→        torch._foreach_mul_(grouped_mu_products, mus)\n   527→\n   528→        torch._foreach_div_(exp_avg_sq_sqrt, bias_correction_sqrt)\n   529→        torch._foreach_add_(exp_avg_sq_sqrt, eps)\n   530→\n   531→        # explicitly delete bias_correction refs to save memory\n   532→        del bias_correction_sqrt\n   533→\n   534→        if capturable:\n   535→            # Build up the step_size multiplier for grad, reusing mus' memory\n   536→            torch._foreach_sub_(mus, 1.0)\n   537→            torch._foreach_mul_(mus, lr)\n   538→            # foreach_sub doesn't allow a scalar as the first arg\n   539→            denom = torch._foreach_sub(grouped_mu_products, 1.0)\n   540→            torch._foreach_neg_(denom)\n   541→            torch._foreach_div_(mus, denom)\n   542→            # - lr * (1 - mu) / (1 - mu_product)\n   543→            step_size_grads = mus\n   544→            # explicitly delete denom to save memory\n   545→            del denom\n   546→\n   547→            # Build up the step_size multiplier for exp_avg, reusing mu_nexts' memory\n   548→            denom = torch._foreach_mul(grouped_mu_products, mu_nexts)\n   549→            torch._foreach_mul_(mu_nexts, lr)\n   550→            # foreach_sub doesn't allow a scalar as the first arg, but it's okay because\n   551→            # we need a negative here anyway\n   552→            torch._foreach_sub_(denom, 1.0)\n   553→            torch._foreach_div_(mu_nexts, denom)\n   554→            # - lr * mu_next / (1 - mu_product * mu_next)\n   555→            step_size_expavg = mu_nexts\n   556→            # explicitly delete denom to save memory\n   557→            del denom\n   558→\n   559→            # we cannot inplace into step_size_grads cuz it is a list of ScalarTensors\n   560→            # and mul'ing with grouped_grads will result in a list of bigger Tensors\n   561→            numerator = torch._foreach_mul(step_size_grads, grouped_grads)\n   562→            torch._foreach_addcmul_(numerator, step_size_expavg, grouped_exp_avgs)\n   563→\n   564→            # finally, update params\n   565→            torch._foreach_addcdiv_(grouped_params, numerator, exp_avg_sq_sqrt)\n   566→        else:\n   567→            step_size_grads = _stack_if_compiling(\n   568→                [\n   569→                    (_get_value(lr) * (1.0 - mu) / (1.0 - _get_value(mu_product))) * -1\n   570→                    for mu_product, mu in zip(grouped_mu_products, mus)\n   571→                ]\n   572→            )\n   573→            step_size_expavg = _stack_if_compiling(\n   574→                [\n   575→                    (\n   576→                        _get_value(lr)\n   577→                        * mu_next\n   578→                        / (1.0 - _get_value(mu_product) * mu_next)\n   579→                    )\n   580→                    * -1\n   581→                    for mu_product, mu_next in zip(grouped_mu_products, mu_nexts)\n   582→                ]\n   583→            )\n   584→\n   585→            torch._foreach_addcdiv_(\n   586→                grouped_params,\n   587→                grouped_grads,\n   588→                exp_avg_sq_sqrt,\n   589→                step_size_grads,  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n   590→            )\n   591→            torch._foreach_addcdiv_(\n   592→                grouped_params,\n   593→                grouped_exp_avgs,\n   594→                exp_avg_sq_sqrt,\n   595→                step_size_expavg,  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n   596→            )\n   597→\n   598→\n   599→@_disable_dynamo_if_unsupported(single_tensor_fn=_single_tensor_nadam)\n   600→def nadam(\n   601→    params: list[Tensor],\n   602→    grads: list[Tensor],\n   603→    exp_avgs: list[Tensor],\n   604→    exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],\n   605→    mu_products: list[Tensor],\n   606→    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n   607→    # kwonly args with defaults are not supported by functions compiled with torchscript issue #70627\n   608→    # setting this as kwarg for now as functional API is compiled by torch/distributed/optim\n   609→    decoupled_weight_decay: bool = False,\n   610→    foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n   611→    capturable: bool = False,\n   612→    differentiable: bool = False,\n   613→    has_complex: bool = False,\n   614→    maximize: bool = False,\n   615→    *,\n   616→    beta1: float,\n   617→    beta2: float,\n   618→    lr: float,\n   619→    weight_decay: float,\n   620→    momentum_decay: float,\n   621→    eps: float,\n   622→):\n   623→    r\"\"\"Functional API that performs NAdam algorithm computation.\n   624→\n   625→    See :class:`~torch.optim.NAdam` for details.\n   626→    \"\"\"\n   627→    if not all(isinstance(t, torch.Tensor) for t in state_steps):\n   628→        raise RuntimeError(\n   629→            \"API has changed, `state_steps` argument must contain a list of singleton tensors\"\n   630→        )\n   631→\n   632→    if not all(isinstance(t, torch.Tensor) for t in mu_products):\n   633→        raise RuntimeError(\n   634→            \"API has changed, `mu_products` argument must contain a list of singleton tensors\"\n   635→        )\n   636→\n   637→    if foreach is None:\n   638→        _, foreach = _default_to_fused_or_foreach(\n   639→            params, differentiable, use_fused=False\n   640→        )\n   641→\n   642→    if foreach and torch.jit.is_scripting():\n   643→        raise RuntimeError(\"torch.jit.script not supported with foreach optimizers\")\n   644→\n   645→    if foreach and {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_015YThkH8oU9K4ViRhemMpR5","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01XDvbNSjHMgiyG3Rgwd3BLr","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/workspace/torch/optim/_foreach.py","content":"# mypy: allow-untyped-defs\nr\"\"\"Shared utilities for foreach optimizer implementations.\"\"\"\n\nfrom typing import cast, Optional\n\nimport torch\nfrom torch import Tensor\n\nfrom .optimizer import _get_capturable_supported_devices, _to_scalar, Optimizer\n\n\n__all__ = [\n    \"check_empty_params\",\n    \"handle_step_updates\",\n    \"apply_maximize\",\n    \"apply_weight_decay\",\n    \"handle_capturable_devices\",\n]\n\n\ndef check_empty_params(params: list[Tensor]) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Check if params list is empty and return early if needed.\n\n    Args:\n        params: List of parameters.\n\n    Returns:\n        True if params is empty, False otherwise.\n    \"\"\"\n    return len(params) == 0\n\n\ndef handle_step_updates(\n    device_state_steps: list[Tensor],\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Apply step updates with CPU optimization.\n\n    Handles the common pattern where step tensors may be on CPU,\n    which requires special handling to avoid expensive kernel launches.\n\n    Args:\n        device_state_steps: List of step tensors for the current device.\n    \"\"\"\n    if len(device_state_steps) == 0:\n        return\n\n    # If compiling, use simple increment\n    if torch.compiler.is_compiling():\n        torch._foreach_add_(device_state_steps, 1)\n    else:\n        # CPU optimization: wrapping 1 once is faster than per-iteration\n        if device_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n            torch._foreach_add_(\n                device_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n            )\n        else:\n            torch._foreach_add_(device_state_steps, 1)\n\n\ndef apply_maximize(\n    device_grads: list[Tensor],\n    maximize: bool,\n) -> list[Tensor]:\n    \"\"\"Apply maximize (gradient negation) if needed.\n\n    Args:\n        device_grads: List of gradients for the current device.\n        maximize: Whether to maximize (negate gradients).\n\n    Returns:\n        Modified gradient list (may be same object if not maximize).\n    \"\"\"\n    if maximize:\n        return torch._foreach_neg(device_grads)  # type: ignore[return-value]\n    return device_grads\n\n\ndef apply_weight_decay(\n    device_grads: list[Tensor],\n    device_params: list[Tensor],\n    weight_decay: float,\n    maximize: bool,\n) -> list[Tensor]:\n    \"\"\"Apply weight decay to gradients.\n\n    This function implements the common pattern where weight decay is applied\n    as gradient penalty. If maximize is True, it reuses the already-allocated\n    modified gradient list for memory efficiency.\n\n    Args:\n        device_grads: List of gradients for the current device.\n        device_params: List of parameters for the current device.\n        weight_decay: Weight decay coefficient.\n        maximize: Whether maximize was applied (affects memory reuse).\n\n    Returns:\n        Modified gradient list.\n    \"\"\"\n    if weight_decay == 0:\n        return device_grads\n\n    # Reuse intermediate memory if maximize was already applied\n    if maximize:\n        torch._foreach_add_(device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay)\n    else:\n        device_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]\n            device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay\n        )\n\n    return device_grads\n\n\ndef handle_capturable_devices(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    capturable: bool,\n    supports_xla: bool = False,\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Validate that all tensors are on supported devices for capturable=True.\n\n    Args:\n        params: List of parameters.\n        state_steps: List of step tensors.\n        capturable: Whether capturable mode is enabled.\n        supports_xla: Whether to support XLA devices.\n\n    Raises:\n        AssertionError: If any tensor is on an unsupported device.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not capturable or torch.compiler.is_compiling():\n        return\n\n    capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices(\n        supports_xla=supports_xla\n    )\n\n    assert all(\n        p.device.type == step.device.type\n        and p.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n        for p, step in zip(params, state_steps)\n    ), (\n        f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: \"\n        f\"{capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n    )\n\n\ndef view_as_real_for_complex(\n    has_complex: bool,\n    *tensor_lists: list[Tensor],\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Convert complex tensors to real view in-place.\n\n    For complex-valued parameters, convert to real representation for\n    foreach operations. This modifies the tensor lists in-place.\n\n    Args:\n        has_complex: Whether any tensors are complex.\n        tensor_lists: Variable number of tensor lists to convert.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not has_complex:\n        return\n\n    # Convert each tensor list in-place\n    for tensor_list in tensor_lists:\n        for i in range(len(tensor_list)):\n            if torch.is_complex(tensor_list[i]):\n                tensor_list[i] = torch.view_as_real(tensor_list[i])\n\n\ndef group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n    tensor_lists: list[list[Tensor]],\n    with_indices: bool = True,\n):\n    \"\"\"Group tensor lists by device and dtype.\n\n    This is a thin wrapper around Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype\n    for easier importing and usage in foreach implementations.\n\n    Args:\n        tensor_lists: List of tensor lists to group.\n        with_indices: Whether to include indices in the result.\n\n    Returns:\n        Dictionary of grouped tensors indexed by (device, dtype).\n    \"\"\"\n    return Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        tensor_lists,  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n        with_indices=with_indices,\n    )\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":10,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1222,"cache_read_input_tokens":51548,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1222},"output_tokens":2,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"0a3a7915-60b6-40df-a6f8-19398147891e","uuid":"a8c1292d-d56d-4610-912a-b443d9966a1d"}

import Tensor\n\nfrom .optimizer import (\n    _default_to_fused_or_foreach,\n    _device_dtype_check_for_fused,\n    _differentiable_doc,\n    _foreach_doc,\n    _fused_doc,\n    _maximize_doc,\n    _params_doc,\n    _to_scalar,\n    _use_grad_for_differentiable,\n    DeviceDict,\n    Optimizer,\n    ParamsT,\n)\n\n\n__all__ = [\"SGD\", \"sgd\"]\n\n\nclass SGD(Optimizer):  # noqa: D101\n    def None,\n    ):  # noqa: D107\n        if isinstance(lr, Tensor) and lr.numel() != 1:\n            raise ValueError(\"Tensor lr must be 1-element\")\n        if lr < 0.0:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid learning rate: {lr}\")\n        if momentum < 0.0:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid momentum value: {momentum}\")\n        if weight_decay < 0.0:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid weight_decay value: {weight_decay}\")\n\n        defaults = {\n            \"lr\": lr,\n            \"momentum\": momentum,\n            \"dampening\": dampening,\n            \"weight_decay\": weight_decay,\n            \"nesterov\": nesterov,\n            \"maximize\": maximize,\n            \"foreach\": foreach,\n            \"differentiable\": differentiable,\n            \"fused\": fused,\n        }\n        if nesterov and (momentum <= 0 or dampening != 0):\n            raise ValueError(\"Nesterov momentum requires a momentum and zero dampening\")\n        super().__init__(params, defaults)\n\n        if fused:\n            self._step_supports_amp_scaling = True\n            self._need_device_dtype_check_for_fused = True\n            if differentiable:\n                raise RuntimeError(\"`fused` does not support `differentiable`\")\n            if foreach:\n                raise RuntimeError(\"`fused` and `foreach` cannot be `True` together.\")\n\n    def __setstate__(self, state):  # noqa: D105\n        super().__setstate__(state)\n        for group in self.param_groups:\n            group.setdefault(\"nesterov\", False)\n            group.setdefault(\"maximize\", False)\n            group.setdefault(\"foreach\", None)\n            group.setdefault(\"differentiable\", False)\n            group.setdefault(\"fused\", False)\n\n    def _init_group(self, group, params, grads, momentum_buffer_list):\n        has_sparse_grad = False\n\n        for p in group[\"params\"]:\n            if p.grad is not None:\n                if group[\"fused\"] and getattr(\n                    self, \"_need_device_dtype_check_for_fused\", True\n                ):\n                    _device_dtype_check_for_fused(p)\n                    self._need_device_dtype_check_for_fused = False\n                params.append(p)\n                grads.append(p.grad)\n                if p.grad.is_sparse:\n                    has_sparse_grad = True\n\n                if group[\"momentum\"] != 0:\n                    state = self.state[p]\n                    momentum_buffer_list.append(state.get(\"momentum_buffer\"))\n\n        return has_sparse_grad\n\n    @_use_grad_for_differentiable\n    def step(self, closure=None):\n        \"\"\"Perform a single optimization step.\n\n        Args:\n            closure (Callable, optional): A closure that reevaluates the model\n                and returns the loss.\n        \"\"\"\n        loss = None\n        if closure is not None:\n            with torch.enable_grad():\n                loss = closure()\n\n        for group in self.param_groups:\n            params: list[Tensor] = []\n            grads: list[Tensor] = []\n            momentum_buffer_list: list[Optional[Tensor]] = []\n\n            has_sparse_grad = self._init_group(\n                group, params, grads, momentum_buffer_list\n            )\n\n            sgd(\n                params,\n                grads,\n                momentum_buffer_list,\n                weight_decay=group[\"weight_decay\"],\n                momentum=group[\"momentum\"],\n                lr=group[\"lr\"],\n                dampening=group[\"dampening\"],\n                nesterov=group[\"nesterov\"],\n                maximize=group[\"maximize\"],\n                has_sparse_grad=has_sparse_grad,\n                foreach=group[\"foreach\"],\n                fused=group[\"fused\"],\n                grad_scale=getattr(self, \"grad_scale\", None),\n                found_inf=getattr(self, \"found_inf\", None),\n            )\n\n            if group[\"momentum\"] != 0:\n                # update momentum_buffers in state\n                for p, momentum_buffer in zip(params, momentum_buffer_list):\n                    state = self.state[p]\n                    state[\"momentum_buffer\"] = momentum_buffer\n\n        return loss\n\n\nSGD.__doc__ = (\n    r\"\"\"Implements stochastic gradient descent (optionally with momentum).\n\n    .. math::\n       \\begin{aligned}\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n            &\\textbf{input}      : \\gamma \\text{ (lr)}, \\: \\theta_0 \\text{ (params)}, \\: f(\\theta)\n                \\text{ (objective)}, \\: \\lambda \\text{ (weight decay)},                          \\\\\n            &\\hspace{13mm} \\:\\mu \\text{ (momentum)}, \\:\\tau \\text{ (dampening)},\n            \\:\\textit{ nesterov,}\\:\\textit{ maximize}                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n            &\\textbf{for} \\: t=1 \\: \\textbf{to} \\: \\ldots \\: \\textbf{do}                         \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\textit{maximize}:                                       \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   -\\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})         \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{else}                                                           \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   \\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})          \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\lambda \\neq 0                                           \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm} g_t \\leftarrow g_t + \\lambda  \\theta_{t-1}                            \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\mu \\neq 0                                               \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm}\\textbf{if} \\: t > 1                                                   \\\\\n            &\\hspace{15mm} \\textbf{b}_t \\leftarrow \\mu \\textbf{b}_{t-1} + (1-\\tau) g_t           \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm}\\textbf{else}                                                          \\\\\n            &\\hspace{15mm} \\textbf{b}_t \\leftarrow g_t                                           \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\textit{nesterov}                                       \\\\\n            &\\hspace{15mm} g_t \\leftarrow g_{t} + \\mu \\textbf{b}_t                               \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm}\\textbf{else}                                                   \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\hspace{15mm} g_t  \\leftarrow  \\textbf{b}_t                                         \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\theta_t \\leftarrow \\theta_{t-1} - \\gamma g_t                    \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\bf{return} \\:  \\theta_t                                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n       \\end{aligned}\n\n    Nesterov momentum is based on the formula from\n    `On the importance of initialization and momentum in deep learning`__.\n    \"\"\"\n    + rf\"\"\"\n    Args:\n        {_params_doc}\n        lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 1e-3)\n        momentum (float, optional): momentum factor (default: 0)\n        dampening (float, optional): dampening for momentum (default: 0)\n        weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay (L2 penalty) (default: 0)\n        nesterov (bool, optional): enables Nesterov momentum. Only applicable\n            when momentum is non-zero. (default: False)\n        {_maximize_doc}\n        {_foreach_doc}\n        {_differentiable_doc}\n        {_fused_doc}\n    \"\"\"\n    + r\"\"\"\n\n    Example:\n        >>> # xdoctest: +SKIP\n        >>> optimizer = torch.optim.SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.1, momentum=0.9)\n        >>> optimizer.zero_grad()\n        >>> loss_fn(model(input), target).backward()\n        >>> optimizer.step()\n\n    __ http://www.cs.toronto.edu/%7Ehinton/absps/momentum.pdf\n\n    .. note::\n        The implementation of SGD with Momentum/Nesterov subtly differs from\n        Sutskever et al. and implementations in some other frameworks.\n\n        Considering the specific case of Momentum, the update can be written as\n\n        .. math::\n            \\begin{aligned}\n                v_{t+1} & = \\mu * v_{t} + g_{t+1}, \\\\\n                p_{t+1} & = p_{t} - \\text{lr} * v_{t+1},\n            \\end{aligned}\n\n        where :math:`p`, :math:`g`, :math:`v` and :math:`\\mu` denote the\n        parameters, gradient, velocity, and momentum respectively.\n\n        This is in contrast to Sutskever et al. and\n        other frameworks which employ an update of the form\n\n        .. math::\n            \\begin{aligned}\n                v_{t+1} & = \\mu * v_{t} + \\text{lr} * g_{t+1}, \\\\\n                p_{t+1} & = p_{t} - v_{t+1}.\n            \\end{aligned}\n\n        The Nesterov version is analogously modified.\n\n        Moreover, the initial value of the momentum buffer is set to the\n        gradient value at the first step. This is in contrast to some other\n        frameworks that initialize it to all zeros. One notable side effect\n        of this decision is that the first momentum value will not be scaled\n        by dampening. Dampening will be applied starting at the second step.\n\n    \"\"\"\n)\n\n\ndef sgd(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    d_p_list: list[Tensor],\n    momentum_buffer_list: list[Optional[Tensor]],\n    # kwonly args with defaults are not supported by functions compiled with torchscript issue #70627\n    # setting this as kwarg for now as functional API is compiled by torch/distributed/optim\n    has_sparse_grad: bool = False,\n    foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n    fused: Optional[bool] = None,\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor] = None,\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor] = None,\n    *,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    momentum: float,\n    lr: float,\n    dampening: float,\n    nesterov: bool,\n    maximize: bool,\n):\n    r\"\"\"Functional API that performs SGD algorithm computation.\n\n    See :class:`~torch.optim.SGD` for details.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Respect when the user inputs False/True for foreach or fused. We only want to change\n    # the default when neither have been user-specified. Note that we default to foreach\n    # and pass False to use_fused. This is not a mistake--we want to give the fused impl\n    # bake-in time before making it the default, even if it is typically faster.\n    if foreach is None and fused is None:\n        # why must we be explicit about an if statement for torch.jit.is_scripting here?\n        # because JIT can't handle Optionals nor fancy conditionals when scripting\n        if not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n            fused, foreach = _default_to_fused_or_foreach(\n                params, differentiable=False, use_fused=False\n            )\n        else:\n            foreach = False\n            fused = False\n    if foreach is None:\n        foreach = False\n    if fused is None:\n        fused = False\n\n    if foreach and torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        raise RuntimeError(\"torch.jit.script not supported with foreach optimizers\")\n    if fused and torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        raise RuntimeError(\"torch.jit.script not supported with fused optimizers\")\n\n    if foreach and not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        func = _multi_tensor_sgd\n    elif fused and not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        func = _fused_sgd\n    else:\n        func = _single_tensor_sgd\n\n    func(\n        params,\n        d_p_list,\n        momentum_buffer_list,\n        weight_decay=weight_decay,\n        momentum=momentum,\n        lr=lr,\n        dampening=dampening,\n        nesterov=nesterov,\n        has_sparse_grad=has_sparse_grad,\n        maximize=maximize,\n        grad_scale=grad_scale,\n        found_inf=found_inf,\n    )\n\n\ndef _single_tensor_sgd(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    momentum_buffer_list: list[Optional[Tensor]],\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\n    *,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    momentum: float,\n    lr: float,\n    dampening: float,\n    nesterov: bool,\n    maximize: bool,\n    has_sparse_grad: bool,\n):\n    assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None\n\n    if not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    for i, param in enumerate(params):\n        grad = grads[i] if not maximize else -grads[i]\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            # Nested if is necessary to bypass jitscript rules\n        if isinstance(lr, Tensor):\n            if lr.requires_grad:\n                param.addcmul_(grad, lr, value=-1)\n            else:\n                param.add_(grad, alpha=-lr)\n        else:\n            param.add_(grad, alpha=-lr)\n\n\ndef _multi_tensor_sgd(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    momentum_buffer_list: list[Optional[Tensor]],\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\n    *,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    momentum: float,\n    lr: float,\n    dampening: float,\n    nesterov: bool,\n    maximize: bool,\n    has_sparse_grad: bool,\n):\n    assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None\n\n    if len(params) == 0:\n        return\n\n    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        [params, grads, momentum_buffer_list],  #
